name: SciVal
description: Allows visualization of research performance, benchmarking relative to peers, identification of new, emerging research trends, and creation of tailored reports. Individual login using an Elsevier online services user account, such as ScienceDirect or Mendeley, is required. See SciVal sign in instructions .

name: CQ Press for Alumni
description: Resources in American government, politics, history, public policy, and current affairs.

name: Sage Knowledge Books
description: Books published by Sage.

name: Sage Knowledge Reference
description: Encyclopedias and handbooks in the social and behavioral sciences.

name: nkoda
description: Sheet music subscription service that can be accessed via app on your phone, tablet or desktop. To get started, download the app on the preferred device(s) and follow the nkoda sign in instructions .

name: Foreign Law Guide
description: Complete bibliographic citations to legislation, the existence of English translations, and selected references to secondary sources.

name: Asia Studies Full-text Online

name: Bloomberg
description: Real-time and historical information on economic conditions, market data and public companies worldwide along with governments, mortgages, money markets, bonds, commodities, stocks, and currencies.

meta.more_info: Access Note: Available on Walker Management Library computers, Calhoun 215 office computer, and the computer on the 4th floor of Central with dual monitors.

name: Business Source Complete
description: Covers most business areas: management, marketing, economics, finance, accounting, & international business. Includes company reports, SWOT analyses, working papers, country reports, industry reports, case studies, etc.

name: VitalLaw
description: Formerly known as Cheetah, this database contains tax and accounting information in all areas. Also contains tools and smart charts.

name: Conference Board

name: Datastream Advance
description: Historical data for financial markets worldwide and an extensive collection of historical country economic and time series data.

name: EconLit with Full Text

name: EIU: Economist Intelligence Unit
description: In-depth reports that include political and economic data and policies. Three separate databases: Country Report, Country Profile, and CountryData.

name: e-Marketer

name: Emerald Insight
description: Scholarly, peer-reviewed journals, books, and case studies from different areas of business, economics, education, engineering, health care, library science, organizational studies, sociology, and more.

name: Encyclopedia of Statistical Sciences
description: Statistical information in anthropology, biology, business, chemistry, communications, earth sciences, economics, education, engineering, government, health, law, music, physics, political science, psychology, religion, sociology, and statistics.

name: Factiva
description: Business magazines, trade publications, and newspapers; includes the Wall Street Journal.

meta.more_info: Logs out after 15 minutes of inactivity. Libguide for Factiva

name: First Research (Intellect)

name: Vault (Business)
description: Industry and career guides, including employer profiles; advice on consulting case interviews, financial interviews, and career trends. Message boards. First-time users must register for a personal account using their vanderbilt.edu email address.

meta.more_info: To access the Jobs area of Vault, students should login via Handshake using their VUNetID. Alumni will have access for 4 years post-graduation.

name: UniWorld
description: Contact information for headquarters, branches, subsidiaries, and affiliates of the multinational firms. Find U.S. corporations with operations in other countries and firms based in other countries with locations in the U.S.

meta.more_info: All current Vanderbilt University students, faculty, staff, and alumni have access to this resource, both on and off-campus. Owen alumni also have access.

name: Hoovers
description: Details on US and international companies, executives, market research reports, and trade publications. Includes financials, subsidiaries, and trade and business news.

name: JSTOR
description: Large and important archive for academic journals, books, and primary sources in the humanities, social sciences, and sciences. Many journals do not have current issues.

name: Gale Directory Library
description: Cross-search Gale directories including: Ward's Business Directory, Encyclopedia of Associations, Scholarships, Fellowships & Loans; Gale Directory of Publications and Broadcast Media; Research Centers Directory; etc.

name: Gartner
description: IT data and analysis: executive summaries, perspectives, research briefs, research notes, special reports on IT-related issues. Spotlights key technologies, products, trends, opportunities, vendors and products.

meta.more_info: Please note that we have a limited subscription with Gartner that does not provide access to all areas of this database, particularly some of the specialized industry research.

name: Passport Euromonitor
description: Euromonitor International's global market analysis software platform, providing industry information, consumer statistics and analysis, historical and forecast data, consumer trends and lifestyles, articles, company profiles, and global reports.

name: IMF eLibrary - Data
description: International statistics related to domestic and international finance. International payments, rates of inflation & deflation, exchange rates, international liquidity, international banking, monetary policy, interest rates, etc. Includes Government Finance Statistics (GFS), Balance of Payments Statistics (BOPS), Direction of Trade Statistics (DOTS), Trade and Investment, and International Financial Statistics (IFS).

meta.more_info: Coverage: Historical data for BOP, DOT, and IFS are available from 1948, and for GFS from 1972.

name: International Index to Black Periodicals
description: International and multidisciplinary coverage of scholarly journals, newsletters, and periodicals from the United States, Africa, and the Caribbean focused on African-Americans, Africa, and African diaspora.

name: iPOLL (Roper Center)
description: Public opinion poll data: poll questions and answers, datasets, and survey questionnaires from academic, commercial, and media polls on opinions and behaviors from major survey research organizations in the United State, including Gallup, Pew, Annenburg Public Policy Center, and news organizations, such as CNN, CBS, ABC, NBC, the New York Times.

name: MarketResearch.com Academic
description: Quantitative and qualitative analysis of the market; primary research and expert analysis, including industry interviews, competitive analysis, market trends, product innovations, buyer behavior, market share, etc.

name: Marquis Who's Who
description: Biographical information for newsworthy American & international figures in the areas of business, education, entertainment, government, health care, law, religion, science and technology, the arts, and sports.

name: Mergent Online
description: Corporate financial histories (including capital changes) and operational histories for international public companies. Customizable reports on companies.

name: Mintel Academic
description: Market research reports focused on consumers in the U.S., U.K., European, and some Asian markets. Market drivers, size & trends and segmentation; supply structure, advertising & promotion, retail distribution, consumer characteristics & forecasts.

name: Polling the Nations
description: Poll questions and responses from national, state, local and special public opinion polls and surveys conducted by polling organizations in the United States and other countries.

name: ProQuest One Academic
description: Scholarly journals, newspapers, dissertations, and business information. Includes: ABI/INFORM Complete, Accounting & Tax, Banking Information Source, Canadian Newsstand Complete, CBCA Complete, Hoover's Company Profiles, OxResearch, Pharmaceutical News Index, Asian Business & Reference, Biology Journals, Career and Technical Education, Computing, Criminal Justice, Education Journals, European Business, Family Health, Health & Medical Complete, Health Management, Military Collection, Newsstand, Nursing & Allied Health Source, Psychology Journals, Religion, Research Library, Science Journals, Social Science Journals, Telecommunications, Snapshots.

name: ProQuest News and Newspapers
description: Direct link to the newspaper section of ProQuest. Includes Alt-Press Watch, Ethnic NewsWatch, Latin American Newsstand, Canadian Newsstand Complete, ProQuest Newsstand, ProQuest Historical Newspapers. Chicago Tribune, Christian Science Monitor, Los Angeles Times, New York Times, Wall Street Journal, Washington Post, Times of London, and The Tennessean.

name: PsycINFO
description: APA PsycINFO is a comprehensive indexing and abstracts of the international psychological literature. Journals, articles, books, dissertations, etc. Subjects include: applied psychology, communication systems, developmental psychology, educational psychology, experimental human and animal psychology, personality, psychological and physical disorders, physiological psychology and neuroscience, professional personnel and issues, psychometrics and statistics, social psychology, social processes and issues, sports psychology and leisure, treatment and prevention.

name: Sports Market Analytics
description: Articles with news and market research information from sports industry sources. Includes market reports on international sporting goods equipment, college sports, endorsements, facilities, fan market profiles, financial reports, licensing, etc.

name: ScienceDirect
description: Scientific, business, technical and medical journals and books, including pre-pub and open access content. The back file of the Business collection is an invaluable archival source for business research.

meta.more_info: You can set up your own personal profile which will enable you to save searches and create alerts for new issues.

name: Social Science Research Network (SSRN)
description: Abstracts on scholarly working papers and forthcoming papers; electronic paper collection containing full-text documents in PDF.

meta.more_info: Access Note: Some titles available to only Law and/or Owen students.

name: Springer Link
description: Journal articles, book chapters, reference works, and protocols.

name: World DataBank: World Development Indicators
description: Collection of time-series data, and global development indicators. Cross-country comparable statistics about development and peoples lives around the globe and includes national, regional and global estimates.

name: Wiley Online Library
description: Journals, books, reference works, laboratory protocols and databases covering life, health and physical sciences, social science, and the humanities.

name: World Bank Open Knowledge Repository
description: Books, journal articles, and working papers on social and economic development. World Bank flagship and annual publications, data, regional and thematic series, training manuals and handbooks, etc.

name: World Biographical Information System (WBIS)

name: WorldCat (FirstSearch)
description: Catalog of books, manuscripts, websites, internet resources, maps, computer programs, musical scores, films, slides, newspapers, journals, magazines, sound recordings, articles, chapters, papers, and videotapes available at libraries worldwide. Cross-search OCLC databases: ArchiveGrid, ArticleFirst, Ebooks, ECO, ERIC, GPO, MEDLINE, OAIster, PapersFirst, ProceedingsFirst, WorldCat, and WorldCatDissertations.

name: World Trade Organization: International Trade and Market Access Data
description: Annual compilation of global trade statistics. Includes detailed analysis and tables (leading traders, trade by sector and product, regional trade, LDCs, etc).

name: ICPSR: Inter-university Consortium for Political and Social Research
description: Social science data for research and instruction along with access to special topic archives sponsored by agencies outside ICPSR, including the National Archive of Criminal Justice Data & the International Archive of Education Data.

meta.more_info: Access Note: For off-campus access to the ICPSR archives, please login: http://proxy.library.vanderbilt.edu/login?url=http://www.icpsr.umich.edu . Vendor Tutorials: Videos on finding and using data from ICPSR are available at https://www.youtube.com/@icpsr .

name: Social Explorer
description: Current and historical census data and demographic information: US Census, American Community Survey, religious membership, carbon emissions data, etc. Customizable maps, charts, and reports.

name: Foundation Directory Online
description: Information on grantmakers and grants. Database of foundations, corporate giving programs, grantmaking public charities, sponsoring companies, recently awarded grants, and recently-filed IRS Forms 990 and 990-PF.

name: CINAHL Ultimate
description: Cumulative Index to Nursing and Allied Health Literature: authoritative coverage of literature related to nursing and allied health professionals.

name: PubMed
description: International journals in medicine, public health, nursing, dentistry, veterinary medicine, and the preclinical sciences. Information from allied health, biological and physical sciences, humanities and information science as they relate to medicine and health care.

name: IMF eLibrary
description: Books, periodicals, working papers, and country reports on the International Monetary Fund's activities, policies, and member countries.

name: GreenFILE (EBSCO host)
description: Information covering all aspects of human impact to the environment. Scholarly, government and general-interest titles on global warming, green building, pollution, sustainable agriculture, renewable energy, recycling, and more.

name: ProQuest Business (including ABI/Inform)
description: Combined search of ABI/INFORM Complete, Accounting & Tax, Banking Information Source, Asian Business and Reference, European Business, and Snapshots.

description: Historical annual reports from North American companies. Company information: financial performance, key officers, competitors, and market trends.

name: Mergent Archives

name: International Abstracts in Operations Research
description: Abstracts in operations research and management science. Index of articles relevant to the philosophy, methodology, or practice of operations research.

You can also adjust your Google Scholar settings to display the Vanderbilt Findit@VU link to appear in search results and allow for access from on or off-campus. Access Google Scholar settings by visiting this page (https://scholar.google.com/scholar_settings). Click library links on the left hand menu. in the search box, search for Vanderbilt and select: Vanderbilt University Library  Findit@VU (with uppercase F), Click Save.

meta.more_info: Add VU to your Google profile: https://scholar.google.com/scholar_settings

name: Hospitality, Tourism and Leisure Collection
description: Industry journals, Fodor's travel guides and magazines covering historical and current affairs in the hospitality and tourism field: cultural aspects of travel, leisure activities, economic aspects of tourism, and more.

name: IBISWorld
description: Large collection of industry reports; market size, competitors, drafting business plans, pitch books, benchmarking, forecasting, business valuations, litigation support, due diligence and more.

name: Gale Business: Insights
description: Company chronologies, company histories, journals, market research reports, market share reports, rankings, recent news, SWOT reports, videos, industry information. Comparison chart tool.

name: PrivCo
description: Business and financial data on major, non-publicly traded corporations: family owned, private equity owned, venture backed, and international unlisted companies. Company financials, M&A deals, valuations, venture capital funding, private equity deals, etc.

National income and production statistics, retail and wholesale trade data, interest rates, economic indicators, and social and demographic statistics, plus data on energy, environmental protection, agriculture, fisheries, crime, etc.

meta.more_info: Coverage: From the U.S. government, 1973 to present; from international intergovernmental, 1983 to present.

name: eBook Collection (EBSCOhost)
description: Large collection of ebooks in many subjects.

meta.more_info: Access Note: Titles restricted to 1 user at a time and become available to other users after 30 minutes of inactivity. Create a user account (click "Sign In" at the top and then "Create a New Account") for many more options, such as saving, folders, and alerts.

name: Checkpoint - Accounting

name: Checkpoint

Daily updates of tax-related news, Treasury Department decisions, federal court decisions, and IRS Revenue Rulings. State, local, and international. To save files and search histories you must register annually for an account .

meta.more_info: Contact the Law Library for more information.

name: Web of Science
description: Citation database from journals and international conference proceedings. Cross-search of the Web of Science Core Collection, BIOSIS Previews, and the SciELO Citation Index (Latin America, Spain, Portugal, the Caribbean and South Africa).

name: Nexis Uni (formerly LexisNexis Academic)
description: News and newspaper stories, federal and state court cases, US law reviews and journals, company profiles and directories. Company Dossier; data on public figures.

meta.more_info: Access Note: Personal account required for some functionality.

name: Peterson's Career Prep
description: Jobs and internship listings; help with resumes and cover letters, tips and advice to explore careers.

name: Forrester
description: Reports and analysis that focus on the business implications of technology change. Jupiter media research and analysis incorporated into Forrester. At the Forrester login screen, enter your Vanderbilt email address in the Business Email Address field without a password, then click the Log In button. You'll be prompted to enter your VU username and password. First time users will need to complete a brief registration form.

meta.more_info: License does not include direct access to Forrester analysts for research inquiries, please do not phone, write, or send e-mail inquiries

name: Gale Business: Entrepreneurship
description: Sample business plans, articles, overviews, directories, business topics and types, "how-to" documents.

description: Extensive selection of statistics for the United States, with selected data for regions, divisions, states, metropolitan areas, cities, and foreign countries from reports and records of government and private agencies.

meta.more_info: Access Note: If prompted to select an account, select Vanderbilt University.

name: EDGAR
description: US government database of public company filings collected by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. Full-text of company annual 10-K reports, quarterly 10-Q reports, proxy statements, prospectus, etc.

name: CultureGrams

name: BCC Research
description: Market research on major economic, scientific, and technological developments in industrial, pharmaceutical, and high technology organizations. Industry analysis and market forecasts for advanced materials, high-tech systems and components, nanotechnology and novel processing methods.

name: American Hospital Directory
description: Lists of hospitals according to ownership, services provided, size, volumes, market area, etc. Search results and hospital reports can be viewed online and downloaded in Excel or PDF formats. Use of this content for any purposes other than education is strictly prohibited.

name: Calcbench
description: Conduct financial statement analysis, financial accounting and auditing using SEC (Security and Exchange Commission) XBRL data. Analyze financial data for public companies with an interactive data platform. Understand competitor financials, identify potential risks to firms, analyze trends across industry sectors.

meta.more_info: Login allows access the databases customization features. Registration requires a valid Vanderbilt email address.

name: ATLA (American Theological Library Association) Religion Database with ATLA Serials PLUS
description: Index to journal articles, book reviews, and collections of essays in all fields of religion. Some full text.

description: Index to journal articles, reports, commentaries, edited works, and obituaries in the fields of social, cultural, physical, biological, and linguistic anthropology, ethnology, archaeology, folklore, material culture, and interdisciplinary studies. Includes materials published in English and European languages from the late 19th century to the present. Compilation of the Royal Anthropological Institutes Anthropological Index and Harvard University's Anthropological Literature databases.

name: Leadership Connect
description: Contact information for companies, government agencies, congressional offices, law firms, news media outlets, healthcare organizations, and nonprofits. Biographical data for American leaders: emails, phone numbers, addresses, and background info.

meta.more_info: Access Note: Exporting to a printer is not available.

name: General OneFile

name: Global Economic Monitor (GEM)

name: Index to Legal Periodicals and Books

name: Opposing Viewpoints in Context (OV or OVIC)

name: Points of View Reference Center

name: Wall Street Journal (ProQuest Historical Newspapers)
description: Historical access to the Wall Street Journal from ProQuest.

name: International Historical Statistics
description: Global statistical data on business, economics, environmental studies, geography, history, politics, sociology, and statistical studies.

name: Alt-PressWatch
description: Newspapers, magazines, and journals covering cultural, economic, political & social change. Highly recommended comprehensive guide to alternative sources of information. Coverage is international and interdisciplinary.

name: Digital National Security Archive
description: Full-text collection of declassified documents relating to U.S. foreign policy decisions from post-World War II to the present.

name: Ethnic NewsWatch
description: Journals, magazines, and newspapers from ethnic and minority presses. National and regional publications: African American, Caribbean, African, Arab, Middle Eastern, Asian/Pacific Islander, European, Eastern European, Hispanic, Jewish, Native People.

name: GenderWatch
description: GenderWatch has been fully incorporated into ProQuest One Women's Studies . Please use ProQuest One Women's Studies to access GenderWatch content along with many other primary and secondary sources on women's studies, gender, and sexuality.

name: Health Technology Assessments
description: Descriptive, non-analytical abstracts of reports on health technologies assessments. There is no rigorous criteria for abstracts to be included in the HTA database.

name: Index Islamicus
description: Bibliography covering all the main Muslim areas of Asia and Africa, as well as Muslims living elsewhere, and their history, beliefs, societies, cultures, languages and literatures. Journal articles, books, reviews, and papers in multi-author volumes.

name: ProQuest Health Management
description: Journals, dissertations and theses on hospitals, insurance, law, statistics, business management, personnel management, ethics, health economics, and public health administration.

name: 17th-18th Century Burney Collection Newspapers
description: English news media from the 17th and 18th centuries: newspapers, pamphlets, and books gathered by the Reverend Charles Burney (1757-1817).

name: Eight Centuries
description: An "index of indexes," for historical scholarly research prior to 1930. Aggregate of indexes to periodicals, newspapers, books, patents, image archives, government documents, etc.

name: 19th Century U.S. Newspapers
description: Digital facsimile images of both full pages and clipped articles for 19th century US newspapers. For each issue, the newspaper is captured from cover-to-cover, providing access to every article, advertisement and illustration.

meta.more_info: Access Note: Available only to Tennessee residents.

name: Academic Onefile
description: Scholarly journals on a broad range of subject areas--arts and the humanities, social sciences, science and technology.

name: History Commons (Accessible Archives)
description: African American Newspapers; The Charleston Mercury; Chester & Delaware Federalist; The Christian Recorder; The Civil War Part I. A Newspaper Perspective; The Colored American; Delaware County American; Delaware County Republican; Frederick Douglass Paper; Douglass Monthly; Freedom's Journal; Media Advertiser; The National Era; The New York Herald; The North Star; The Pennsylvania Gazette; The Pennsylvania Genealogical Catalog [collection]; Pennsylvania Newspaper Record; The Pennsylvania Packet; Porcupines Gazette; Provincial Freeman; Richmond Enquirer; The Upland Union; Village Record; Weekly Advocate.

name: ACLS Humanities E-Book
description: American Council of Learned Societies's collection of ebooks in the humanities.

name: ACM Digital Library
description: Association for Computing Machinery's access to journals, magazines, transactions, ACM proceedings, SIG proceedings, newsletters, and publications by affiliated organizations.

name: Library Search
description: Check out the Library Search which finds print and electronic items, including journal and newspaper articles, book chapters, books, reviews, legal documents, and much more. Note: Due to licensing restrictions, some content is only available to current students, faculty, and staff.

name: Ad Access
description: Image database of advertisements printed in U.S. and Canadian newspapers and magazines between 1911 and 1955. Subject areas: Radio, Television, Transportation, Beauty and Hygiene, and World War II.

name: AfricaBib.org: African Women
description: Books and government documents; articles from books, periodicals, and journals; theses and dissertations; conference papers; and videocassettes.

name: African American Experience
description: Articles, audio clips, images, primary source documents, maps, monographs, and government documents covering all aspects of the African American experience from Greenwood Press, Negro University Press, etc.

name: African American Historical Serials Collection
description: Newspapers, magazines, reports, and annuals from various African American organizations including churches and educational and service institutions.

name: African American Music Reference
description: Text and liner notes of blues, jazz, spirituals, civil rights songs, slave songs, minstrelsy, rhythm and blues, gospel, and other forms of black American musical expression.

name: African American Newspapers (Accessible Archives)

Newspapers are: The Christian Recorder (18611902), The Colored American (18371841), Frederick Douglass Paper (18511855; 18591863) , Freedoms Journal (18271829), The National Era (18471860), The North Star (18471851), Provincial Freeman (18541857), Weekly Advocate (18371837)

name: African American Newspapers (ProQuest)
description: Primary source material for the study of American history and African-American culture, history, politics, and the arts. Includes: Atlanta Daily World (1931-2003), Baltimore Afro-American (1893-1988), Chicago Defender (1909-1975), New York Amsterdam News (1922-1993), Los Angeles Sentinel (1934-2005), Norfolk Journal and Guide (1916-2003), The Philadelphia Tribune (1912-2001), and Pittsburgh Courier (1911-2002).

name: African American Newspapers (Readex)
description: Extensive African American newspaper archives. Includes the Wisconsin Historical Society, Kansas State Historical Society, Freedom's Journal (NY), The Colored Citizen (OH), Rights of All (NY), Wisconsin Afro-American, New York Age, Virginia Journal and Alexandria Advertiser, Richmond Planet, Cleveland Gazette, The Appeal (MN), etc.

name: African American Periodicals
description: Holdings of the Wisconsin Historical Society. Based upon James P. Danky's African-American Newspapers and Periodicals: A National Bibliography (Harvard, 1998).

name: AGRICOLA
description: National Agricultural Library's catalog. Publications and resources encompassing all aspects of agriculture and allied disciplines, including animal and veterinary sciences, entomology, plant sciences, forestry, aquaculture and fisheries, farming and farming systems, agricultural economics, extension and education, food and human nutrition, and earth and environmental sciences.

name: Alim
description: Key Islamic documents in both Arabic and English. Audio recordings of the Quran.

name: Almanac of American Politics (AAP)
description: Biographical and political summaries of elected officials, campaign expenditures, voting records, interest-group ratings, and congressional staff look-ups. In-depth overviews of each state and house district, demographic data, analysis of voting trends, and political histories.

meta.more_info: Coverage: We have access to current member information only.

name: America: History and Life
description: Index of literature covering the history and culture of the United States and Canada. Journal articles, book titles, book reviews, and dissertations.

name: American Bibliography of Slavic, East European, and Eurasian Studies (ABSEEES)
description: Information on East-Central Europe and the former Soviet Union.

name: American Chemical Society Publications
description: American Chemical Society's peer-reviewed research journals in chemical and related sciences.

name: Open Dissertations

name: Explore Census Data

name: American Memory

name: American National Biography Online

name: American State Papers, 1789-1838
description: Congressional and Executive Department publications. Speeches and messages of Presidents Washington, Adams, Jefferson, and Madison.

name: America's Historical Newspapers
description: Cover-to-cover reproductions of early American newspapers offers an unprecedented look back into the history of the United Statesthe story of its people, ideals, commerce, and everyday life. Includes the following collections: African American Newspapers Series 1 & 2, American Religion: Denominational Newspapers, Caribbean Newspapers, Early American Newspapers, Series 1: From Colonies to Nation Early American Newspapers, Series 2: The New Republic, Early American Newspapers: 1690-1789, Early American Newspapers: 1780-1789, and Hispanic American Newspapers.

name: Anne Philologique
description: lndex for research in Classics. Entries cover articles, books, book reviews, Festschrifte , and other collections dealing with the ancient Greek and Roman world, from the second millennium BCE to approximately 800 CE.

name: Annual Bibliography of English Language and Literature, 1920- (ABELL)

English culture: custom, belief, narrative, song, dance, and material culture.

name: Annual Reviews
description: Critical reviews written by leading scientists in the biomedical, life, physical, and social sciences including economics. Annual Reviews are highly cited in ISI's Journal Citation Reports.

name: AnthroSource
description: Archive of all journals, newsletters, and bulletins of the American Anthropological Association.

name: AP Newsroom
description: Associated Press photographs, audio, graphics, news stories, and headlines.

name: Art Full Text and Art Index Retrospective

name: ARTFL Project
