description: International journal on the study of German Language and Literature, including peripheral fields. Monographic studies, collected volumes, and journals.

name: Global Music Archive
description: Traditional and popular song, music, and dance of Africa and the Americas. Includes the Digital Collection of East African Recordings and the Appalachian Dulcimer Archive.

name: Goethes Werke im WWW
description: Weimar Edition of Goethe's collective works. Includes all illustrations, variants, indexes, and commentary. In German.

name: GPO (Government Printing Office) Catalog, 1976-
description: All types of US government documents, including Congressional reports, hearings, debates, and records; judiciary materials; and documents issued by executive departments (Defense, State, Labor, Office of the President, etc.).

name: Grove Dictionary of Art / Grove Art Online
description: Scholarly encyclopedia devoted to the visual arts: painting, sculpture, architecture, photography, drawing, and printmaking, as well as decorative arts. Multicultural approach offers detailed analyses of civilizations from Asia, Africa, the Americas, Europe, and the Pacific, including art forms, cultures, and artists. Provides links to color images.

name: Gutenberg-e
description: Ebooks with extensive documentation, links to supplementary literature, images, music, video, and related web sites.

name: Handbook of Chemistry and Physics (CRC)
description: Electronic version of the CRC Handbook of Chemistry and Physics. Includes many updated and expanded tables: Fundamental Physical Constants (the new set of CODATA recommended values, replacing the 1986 set); The Elements (descriptive texts on the occurrence, properties, history, and uses of all the chemical elements); Dissociation Constants of Organic Acids and Bases (expanded by 50%); Dipole Moments (revised and expanded); Threshold Limits for Airborne Contaminants (including the most recent recommendations).

name: Handbook of Latin American Studies
description: Primary database for research on Latin America, indexing books and articles on Latin America in the social sciences and humanities. Covers the principal research worldwide and is especially strong in its inclusion of Latin American publications, articles, etc. Includes abstracts. Includes unpublished volumes in draft form.

name: HAPI: Hispanic American Periodicals Index
description: Principal index to articles on Latin America and Hispanic Americans in the US; journals on Latin American and Caribbean themes in the social sciences and humanities published in the Western world. Book reviews also indexed.

name: HarpWeek (Harper's Weekly)
description: Contains scanned images of Harper's Weekly Magazine 1857-1912, with full indexing. Includes all illustrations, cartoons, news, literature, editorials, and advertisements.

name: Hartford Institute for Religion Research
description: Sociological research in the area of religion: women and religion, congregations, religion and the family, etc. Articles, bibliographies, syllabi, and other scholarly resources.

name: HathiTrust
description: Digitized collections of some of the nations great research libraries, established in this repository to archive and share their digital content.

meta.more_info: Access Note: VU is a partner institution. Login is required for downloading. Tutorial: https://researchguides.library.vanderbilt.edu/HathiTrustAccess

name: Health and Wellness (Gale)
description: Medical reference materials: journals, pamphlets, videos, and articles from general interest publications and reference titles.

name: Health and Medicine (Gale OneFile)
description: Health information resources: documents, articles, and book excerpts from nursing and allied health journals and other publications.

name: HeinOnline
description: Comprehensive legal research database. Extensive coverage of law reviews and historical access to resources such as the Federal Register, the Code of Federal Regulations, and US Supreme Court decisions. Legislative history and select foreign and international materials. Contains specialized libraries: intellectual property, taxation and economic reform, etc.

name: HeritageQuest Online
description: Collection of genealogical and historical sources, with coverage dating back to the 1700s. Searches U.S. Federal Censuses, genealogy and local history books, Periodical Source Index (PERSI), Revolutionary War records, Freedman's Bank Records, slave records (from either the 1850 or 1860 US Census) including counts of enslaved people by state, and the Memorials, Petitions and Private Relief Actions from the U.S. Serial Set.

name: Historic Documents
description: Primary sources covering current events around the world.

name: Historical Abstracts
description: Index of academic historical journals on the history of the world (excluding the United States and Canada) from 1450 to the present, including world history, military history, women's history, history of education, etc.

name: Historical Photographs of the Land of Israel
description: Collection of photographs documenting people, events, and places in pre-state Israel during the 19th and 20th centuries. Includes visual documentations of: streets and cities, 1948 Arab-Israeli War, the Hebrew Reali School in Haifa, Jewish social life and culture during the 19th century, etc.

name: Historical Statistics of the United States: Millennial Edition (HSUS)
description: Historical statistics on social, political, and economic aspects of the United States. Census and other statistical data. Create custom tables and merge columns from multiple tables to create custom tables, which can also be downloaded, printed, or graphed. Essays by experts place topics such as migration, slavery, health, crime, Native Americans, and the Confederate States of America in historical context.

meta.more_info: If you experience an error message, please clear your browser's cache and retry connecting.

name: History of Science, Technology, and Medicine
description: Journal articles, conference proceedings, books, dissertations, serials, maps and other related materials covering topics on the role of scientific discovery in society and culture, and the historiography of scientific disciplines from prehistory to the present.

name: History Vault
description: Digitized letters, papers, photographs, scrapbooks, financial records, diaries, and other primary source materials.  American Indians and the American West  American Politics and Society  Civil Rights and the Black Freedom Struggle  International Relations and Military Conflicts  Southern Life, Slavery, and the Civil War  Women's Studies  Workers and Labor Unions

name: Homeland Security Digital Library
description: Documents related to homeland security policy, strategy, and organizational management from a wide variety of sources including federal, state, and local governments; international governments and institutions; nonprofit organizations; and private entities.

name: House and Senate Journals, Series I, 1789-1817
description: Journals of the United States House and Senate proceedings, record the minutes of floor action. Chronicles matters considered on the floor, votes and other actions taken, and excerpts from executive department communications and member responses.

name: U.K. Parliamentary Papers
description: Historical and current working documents from the House of Commons and the House of Lords, including papers, debates, journals, bills, and acts. Also included are command papers, Hansard (Official Report of debates), and public petitions to Parliament.

name: IBFD Tax Research Platform
description: Global Tax Analyses, Global Tax Surveys, International Tax Glossary, Tax News Service, Tax Treaties Database, and Tax Treaty Case Law by an international provider of cross-border tax expertise and independent tax research.

name: ICLR
description: Law reports published by the Incorporated Council of Law Reporting for England and Wales.

name: IEEE Xplore
description: Access to IEEE and IEE transactions, journals, magazines and conference proceedings published since 1988 and all current IEEE standards. Engineering.

name: Independent Voices
description: Alternative press journals, magazines, and newspapers covering feminists, dissident GIs, campus radicals, Native Americans, anti-war activists, Black Power advocates, Latinx, gays, lesbians, etc.

name: Index of Medieval Art
description: Records works of art produced from early apostolic times up to A.D. 1550 (extended in the case of the Morgan and Princeton Library projects to include this manuscript holdings up to the middle of the sixteenth century). Particular emphasis and focus on art of the western world until recent years. Both text and images included when possible.

name: Index Religiosus
description: Includes "Revue d'histoire ecclsiastique" and "Brepolis Religion  Bibliographies and Encyclopaedias," Covers articles, books, and reviews that deal with different aspects of the Churchs history (institutions, orders, congregations, people, hagiography, etc.). Not limited to church history; also takes into account political history, social and economic history, archaeology, history of art, music, architecture, relations with Islam and Judaism, etc.

name: Index Theologicus (IxTheo)
description: Periodicals in theological and religious studies, festschriften and congress publications. Replaces and expands the Zeitschrift Inhaltsdienst Theologie (ZID).

name: Index to Foreign Legal Periodicals
description: Legal literature covering foreign (non-Anglo-American) law, and comparative law and legal systems. Journal articles, congress reports, essay collections, yearbooks, and book reviews.

name: Index to Hebrew Periodicals
description: Consists of four parts: Index to Hebrew Periodicals (major Hebrew journals from 1977 to date); Eretz Israel Database (non-Hebrew articles of all periods, pre-1977 Hebrew articles, and a selection of books, pamphlets, and reports, all focused on the history, geography, and archaeology of Israel); Tel-Hai Index to Hebrew Newspapers (various newspapers from 1985-1992 and 1994-1997); Bar-Ilan Indexing Project (articles in the literary supplements of the daily Hebrew press from 1985 to present as well as indexes to other Hebrew periodicals.)

meta.more_info: English interface: http://proxy.library.vanderbilt.edu/login?url=http://lib.haifa.ac.il/systems/ihpeng.html . (You can also toggle between Hebrew and English using the dropdown on the upper right of each page.

name: Index to Jewish Periodicals
description: Index on Jewish history, activity and thought. Comprehensive guide to articles, book reviews, and feature stories in journals devoted to Jewish affairs.

name: Information on National Statistical Systems
description: Links to national statistical offices provided by the United Nations. Country profiles include a brief history of the country's statistical system, legal basis, the statistical program, etc.

name: Informe Acadmico
description: Popular Hispanic magazines (e.g., Fem, Hemisphere, Hola, Hispanic Times, Latin Beat, Latinas, PC, Proceso, Semana). Selected pamphlets in areas such as health care. Spanish thesaurus.

name: News (Gale OneFile)
description: National, regional, and local newspapers; images; radio and television broadcasts and transcripts.

name: IntellectDiscover
description: Scholarly research articles, books, monographs, reports, statistics, etc.

name: INSPEC
description: Physics, computer science, electrical engineering, and electronics; citations and abstracts to journals, conference proceedings and other publications in Physics, Electrical Engineering, Electronics and Computing. Includes access to Inspec Analytics .

meta.more_info: Vendor Tutorial: Inspec Analytics User Guide

name: International Bibliography of Art (IBA)
description: Resource for scholarly literature on western art, and successor to the Bibliography of the History of Art (BHA).

name: International Bibliography of the Social Sciences (IBSS)
description: Broad coverage of international publications, including over 100 languages and countries. Indexes journal articles, books, reviews, and selected chapters.

name: International Encyclopaedia of Laws
description: Comprehensive reports for countries on: commercial & economic law, private international law, tort law, insurance law, labour law, intellectual property, constitutional law, contracts, social security, environmental law, civil procedure, family & succession, etc.

name: Music Periodicals Database
description: Indexing and abstracts for music periodicals; includes including music education, performance, ethnomusicology, musical theatre, theory, popular music forms and composition.

name: International Medieval Bibliography
description: Interdisciplinary bibliography of the European Middle Ages, covering Europe, the Middle East and North Africa. Periodicals and miscellany volumes (conference proceedings, essay collections, Festschriften, and exhibition catalogues).

name: International Monetary Fund (IMF) Publications
description: Links to the full text of recent IMF working papers, staff country reports, papers on policy analysis and assessment, and member country publications.

name: International Tables for Crystallography
description: Articles and tables of data relevant to crystallographic research and to applications of crystallographic methods in all sciences concerned with the structure and properties of materials. Symmetry, diffraction methods and techniques of crystal structure determination, and the physical and chemical properties of crystals. Discussions of theory, practical explanations, and examples.

name: Investment Arbitration Reporter (IAReporter)
description: Tracks international investment arbitrations between foreign investors and their host states. Investigates and reports on public and non-public claims; digests and analyzes jurisprudence and awards (both public and non-public); and offers a dataset for empirical international law research.

name: Investment Claims
description: Materials and analysis in international investment law and arbitration. Arbitration awards and decisions, bilateral investment treaties, multilateral treaties, journal articles, monographs, and arbitration laws.

name: IPA Source
description: International Phonetic Alphabet (IPA) transcriptions and literal translations of opera arias and art song texts.

name: Iter: Gateway to the Middle Ages and Renaissance
description: Bibliography of articles, essays, books and reviews. Also: a directory of scholars, an online edition of Paul Oskar Kristeller's Iter Italicum, access to the journal Renaissance Quarterly, and the Baptisteria Sacra.

name: Jacoby Online
description: Revised edition of the Greek texts of Felix Jacobys Die Fragmente der Griechischen Historiker. English translations of the Greek fragments, critical commentary, and a brief encyclopedia-style entry about each historians life and works, with a select bibliography.

name: Jazz Discography
description: Information about jazz records, bandleaders and recording sessions: leader/group name; album title; musicians, with instruments played; location and date of recording session; tune names, including "matrix" and release numbers.

meta.more_info: Access Note: Only available on-campus. Login with VUNetID. Coverage: 1896 to present.

name: JewishEncyclopedia.com
description: Complete and unedited articles and illustrations of the 12-volume Jewish Encyclopedia, published between 1901-1906.

name: Johns Hopkins Guide to Literary Theory and Criticism
description: Historical survey of critics and theorists, critical and theoretical schools and movements, and the critical and theoretical innovations of specific countries and historical periods. Concentrates heavily on modern criticism and theoretical research.

name: JOLIS
description: Catalog of the International Monetary Fund and World Bank libraries. Covers business, agriculture, trade, energy, natural resources, monetary policy, labor and finance.

name: Journal Citation Reports
description: Provides a systematic and objective means of determining the relative importance of science and social sciences journals within their respective subject categories.

name: vLex Justis
description: Full-text legal library database of case law and legislation as well as a citator, providing authoritative coverage of over 130 jurisdictions. Formerly JustisOne.

name: Kafkas Werke im WWW
description: Critical edition of Kafka's complete works, Franz Kafka, Kritische Ausgabe, Schriften und Tagebcher. German version and English version.

name: King James Bible (1611)
description: Text of the King James Bible, 1611 edition, including all introductory matter, annotation, calendars, genealogies and tables.

name: Kluwer Arbitration
description: International commercial arbitration research. Expert commentary and primary source materials. Includes case law, commentary, conventions, legislation and rules. Investment Arbitration section. First-time users must register for a Kluwer account with their @vanderbilt.edu email.

name: Kluwer Intellectual Property
description: KluwerIP provides information on international intellectual property law. It includes access to books, journals, and practice tools. It also is a resource for international intellectual property law news. First-time users must register for a Kluwer account with their @vanderbilt.edu email.

name: Knovel (Online Interactive Science and Engineering Books and Databases)
description: Reference books and databases from sci-tech publishers.**Off campus access to Knovels content requires you to register for Knovel temporarily. Use this link: http://app.knovel.com/web/register.html?orgregkey=22cc49dd-0b5f-4acb-9dce-aa33a4219721 and complete a short registration form. Register with your Vanderbilt email address. Once you activate your account, you will have off-campus access to Knovel.

meta.more_info: Access Note: Users must login from off campus using a Knovel account- see instructions above.

name: Knovel Critical Tables
description: This interactive Knovel reference contains tables of physical, solvent, and thermodynamic properties. The physical property tables include over 21,000 inorganic and organic compounds, and pure substances. The solvent property tables have 385 solvents, and the thermodynamic property tables have data for over 15,000 compounds.

name: LANIC: Latin American Network Information Center
description: One of the largest guides for information about Latin American. Provides Latin American users with access to academic databases and information services, and to provide Latin Americanists around the world with access to information on and from Latin America.

name: Latin American Newsstream
description: Principal newspapers from most Latin American countries and many newspapers from Puerto Rico. Regional news, business, sports and the arts, regional wire services and magazines. In Spanish and/or Portuguese.

name: Latin American Periodicals Tables of Contents (LAPTOC)
description: Tables of contents of academic and research journals published in Latin America and the Caribbean.

name: Latinnews.com
description: Political, economic, and security analysis on Latin America and the Caribbean.

meta.more_info: Coverage: 1967-present Access Note: When you are finished, please log off the site FIRST before closing the window.

name: Law.com (Law users only)
description: Online access to publications from American Lawyer Media (ALM). This includes: The American Lawyer, The National Law Journal, New York Law Journal, Supreme Court Brief, Corporate Counsel, Legaltech News. Legal Week, Inside Counsel, The Recorder, and others.

meta.more_info: Access Note: Users who have a LexisNexis login may also use those credentials to access law.com.

name: Law360 (Law users only)
description: Law360 is a legal news source that covers major litigation in U.S. federal courts, the world's 2,000 top companies, and every major development in class actions and multidistrict litigation. It also provides daily topical e-mail newsletters as well as key documents underlying reported litigation.

name: Left Index
description: Political, economic, social and culturally engaged scholarship. Citations and abstracts (with some full text).

name: Legal Scholarship Network
description: Social Science Research Network's legal area, providing access to accepted and working papers.

meta.more_info: For SSRN email alert services, please contact the Law Library .

name: LegalTrac
description: Indexing for major law reviews, legal newspapers, bar association journals, and international legal journals. Law-related articles from business and general interest titles.

name: Lexis+
description: Legal research system for Vanderbilt Law School faculty, students and staff. Please see a reference librarian for assistance.

name: Library and Information Science Abstracts (LISA)
description: Covers all aspects of librarianship, library users, information retrieval, and more. International in scope.

name: Library of Latin Texts - Series A & B
description: Series A includes literature from antiquity, patristic authors, Middle Ages, and neo-Latin literature. Series B includes Latin texts of all genres and all periods: medical and veterinary texts from Late Antiquity, from east-central European historians, east-central European authors, and works from authors of the Italian and Northern renaissances.

name: Library, Information Science & Technology Abstracts
description: Index of journals, books, research reports and proceedings on librarianship, classification, cataloging, bibliometrics, online information retrieval, information management, etc.

name: Linguistics and Language Behavior Abstracts (LLBA)
description: Abstracts and indexing of all areas of linguistics. Applied linguistics, psycholinguistics, history and philosophy of language, syntax and morphology, lexicography, sociolinguistics, nonverbal communication, phonetics, learning disabilities, hearing and speech physiology.

name: LiteraryMarketPlace.com
description: Resource for book publishing industry information. Listings of US and international publishers, information for literary agents, trade services, sales/distribution resources and other categories of book publishing information.

name: Literature Criticism (Gale)
description: Biographical essays; critical essays or excerpts taken from books, magazines, literary reviews, newspapers, scholarly journals, and interviews with featured authors. Includes ten publications: Contemporary Literary Criticism, Nineteenth Century Literary Criticism, Twentieth Century Literary Criticism, Poetry Criticism, Short Story Criticism, Drama Criticism, Classical and Medieval Literature Criticism, Shakespearean Criticism, Literature Criticism from 1400-1800, and Children's Literature Review.

name: Literature Online
description: Literary criticism, selected full-text works (poetry, poems, and drama), author pages, reviews, and reference sources including biographies, bibliographies, and encyclopedias.

name: LLMC Digital
description: Historical legal information from the federal government, US states and territories, foreign jurisdictions, and international organizations. Access to broad collections, such as the Anglo-American and Multi-Jurisdictional subject collections.

name: Loeb Classical Library
description: Greek and Latin literature. Epic and lyric poetry, tragedy and comedy, history, travel, philosophy, oratory, medical writers and mathematicians; and Church Fathers who made particular use of pagan culture. Latin, Greek, and English texts.

name: London Low Life
description: Rare books, ephemera, maps, periodicals, and other materials relating to 18th, 19th and early 20th century London. Chronology, interactive maps, essays, online galleries and links. Fast literature, street ephemera (posters, advertising, playbills, ballads and broadsides), penny fiction, cartoons, Tallis Street Views, chapbooks, Old London Street Cries, Swells guides to London prostitution, gambling and drinking dens, tourist guides, and topography, manuscripts of George Gissing.

name: Luthers Werke im WWW
description: Complete collection of Martin Luther's writings based on die Weimar Edition. German version and English version.

name: Making of America (Cornell)
description: Primary sources in American social history from the antebellum period through reconstruction. The collection is particularly strong in the subject areas of education, psychology, American history, sociology, religion, and science and technology. Separate sites at Cornell and the University of Michigan contain different sets of materials.

name: Making of America (Michigan)
description: Primary sources in American social history from the antebellum period through reconstruction. The collection is particularly strong in the subject areas of education, psychology, American history, sociology, religion, and science and technology.

name: Making of Modern Law: Foreign Primary Sources, 1600-1970
description: Historical legal codes, statutes, regulations, and commentaries on codes from the United Kingdom, France, Germany, and other countries in northern Europe. Crucial sources of historical statutes and regulations for the UK and the Republic of Ireland.

name: Making of Modern Law: Foreign, Comparative & International Law, 1600-1926
description: Treatises and similar monographs in: international law; comparative law; foreign law; Roman law; Islamic law; Jewish law; and ancient law.

name: Making of Modern Law: Legal Treatises, 1800-1926
description: Digital images of legal treatises on British Commonwealth and American law. Coverage includes casebooks, local practice manuals, books on legal form, works for lay readers, pamphlets, letters, and speeches.

name: Making of Modern Law: Primary Sources, 1620-1926
description: State and municipal codes, documents relating to constitutional conventions, and other resources in American legal history.

name: Making of Modern Law: Trials, 1600-1926
description: Provides access to historical documents relating to famous trials during this time period.

name: Making of Modern Law: U.S. Supreme Court Records and Briefs, 1832-1978
description: Records and briefs brought before the US Supreme Court.

name: Making of the Modern World
description: Digital facsimile images of unique primary sources that track the development of the modern, western world through the lens of trade and wealth. History, political science, social conditions, technology and industry, economics, area studies, etc.

name: MathSciNet: Mathematical Reviews (American Mathematical Society)
description: Pure and applied mathematics and statistics, and applications to other fields, such as physics, computer science, and engineering. Most entries contain an author's abstract or critical review.

name: Max Planck Encyclopedia of Public International Law
description: Comprehensive database on articles on every aspect of public international law. Primary documents, such as treaties, are also available for some entries.

name: Medline (Web of Science)
description: US National Library of Medicine premier life sciences database. Biomedicine and life sciences, bioengineering, public health, clinical care, and plant and animal science. Search precisely with MeSH terms and CAS registry numbers; link to NCBI databases and PubMed.

name: Mental Measurements Yearbook with Tests in Print
description: Guide to testing instruments. Contains information essential for a complete evaluation of test products within such diverse areas as psychology, education, business, and leadership. Comprehensive bibliography to all known commercially available tests that are currently in print in English. Includes test purpose, test publisher, in-print status, price, test acronym, intended test population, administration times, publication date(s), and test author(s).

name: Met Opera on Demand
description: Full-length Met performances, including HD videos from the Met's series of movie theater transmissions, classic telecasts, and radio broadcasts. Subtitles and synopsis.

name: Middle Eastern and Central Asian Studies (MECAS)
description: Bibliographic index of research, policy and scholarly discourse on the countries and peoples of the Middle East, Central Asia and North Africa.

name: Middle English Compendium
description: Middle English Dictionary, a HyperBibliography of Middle English prose and verse, and a Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse. Also gives access to a large collection of Middle English texts and other resources.

name: MIML: Musical Instruction and Musical Learning, 1450-1650
description: Bibliography of secondary literature on musical instruction and musical learning during the Medieval and Early Modern periods.

name: MLA International Bibliography
description: Subject indexing of journals, books, proceedings, and dissertations in the areas of literature, language and linguistics, folklore, film, literary theory & criticism, dramatic arts, and the historical aspects of printing and publishing.

name: Nation Archive
description: Complete archive of The Nation, Americas oldest weekly magazine and one of the countrys definitive journalistic voices of writing on politics, culture, books, and the arts.

meta.more_info: Coverage: July 6, 1865 to present. User Limit: Unlimited.

name: National Bureau of Economic Research (NBER) Working Papers and Publications
description: National Bureau of Economic Research makes results of research available in preliminary form to encourage discussion and suggestions for revision before publication.

name: National Journal
description: Nonpartisan reporting on the current political environment and emerging policy trends.

name: National Security Archive
description: Documents that have been declassified and organized into Electronic Briefing Books. Each document accompanied by identifying information and a summary of its significance.

meta.more_info: Coverage: Post World War II to present.

name: Naxos Music Library
description: Streaming audio service of the complete recording catalogs of the Naxos label and numerous other labels. The main genres covered are classical, world, folk, jazz, and Chinese music.

meta.more_info: Access Note: Please remember to "log out" once you are done (use red log out button). Closing your browser window does not log you out of the system, and could prevent another user from accessing Naxos.

name: NCBI Databases
description: A molecular sequence retrieval system developed at the National Center for Biotechnology Information that provides integrated access to both nucleotide and protein sequence records and medline citations.

name: NCJRS (National Criminal Justice Reference Service) Abstracts Database
description: Corrections, courts, crime prevention, criminal justice statistics, drugs and crime, international resources, juvenile justice, law enforcement, research and evaluation, and victims. Index, abstracts, and some full-text.

name: New Palgrave Dictionary of Economics Online
description: Essays covering a broad range of topics, methodologies, and theories within the field of economics. Many articles contributed by "eminent economists" writing about their area of expertise.

name: New Pauly
description: Encyclopaedic coverage of the second millennium BCE to early medieval Europe; with special emphasis on the interaction between Greco-Roman culture and Semitic, Celtic, Germanic, and Slavonic culture, and ancient Judaism, Christianity, and Islam. Also includes the aftermath of antiquity and the process of continuous reinterpretation and revaluation of ancient heritage, and the history of classical scholarship.

name: New Testament Abstracts
description: Abstracts of periodical articles, reviews of books and software, and summaries of books focused on the New Testament. Periodical abstracts cover publications in many languages.

name: New Testament Gateway
description: Directory of annotated links on topics connected with the academic study of the New Testament and Christian origins. Bibliographies of articles, books, dissertations for some topics, and links to course materials.

name: New York Times (ProQuest Historical Newspapers)
description: First-hand accounts and unparalleled coverage of the politics, society and events of the time.

name: NIST Chemistry WebBook
description: Chemical and physical property data. Thermochemical and thermophysical properties as well as IR spectra, mass spectra, UV/Vis spectra.

name: NIST/TRC Web Thermo Tables
description: Thermodynamic property data for pure compounds with a primary focus on organics.

name: NTIS (National Technical Information Service)
description: Locate and order government publications (technical reports) and other products issued by National Technical Information Service. Searching capability is limited to titles and topics. Most records do NOT include product summaries (abstracts).

name: NYPL Digital Gallery
description: Digital images of drawings, illuminated manuscripts, maps, photographs, posters, prints, rare illustrated books, etc. representing the applied sciences, fine and decorative arts, history, performing arts, and social sciences.

name: Odum Institute Archive
description: Poll question text, study information, and frequency distributions.

name: OED: Oxford English Dictionary
description: The accepted authority on the English language. Guide to the meaning, history, and pronunciation of English words traced through quotations.

name: Official Documents Systems (ODS) of the United Nations
description: Multilingual database containing full text of United Nations official documentation from 1993, with older publications added continuously. Includes full text of resolutions of the General Assembly, Security Council, Economic and Social Council, and the Trusteeship Council from 1946, as well as selective United Nations administrative issuances. The system does not contain press releases, UN sales publications, the United Nations Treaty Series or information brochures issued by the Department of Public Information. Documents are stored in portable document format (PDF).

name: Old Testament Abstracts
description: Indexing and abstracts for journal articles, monographs, multi-author works, and software related to Old Testament studies. Topics covered include antiquities, archaeology, biblical theology, philology, etc.

name: Institute for the Study of Ancient Cultures
description: Materials on the history of the ancient Near East from prehistoric times through the Late Antique period.

name: Oxford African American Studies Center
description: Articles primary source documents, images, maps, charts, tables, and government documents from five Oxford University Press encyclopedias and reference sources. Also includes The African American National Biography .

name: Oxford Art Online
description: Oxfords art reference works: Grove Art Online, the Benezit Dictionary of Artists, the Encyclopedia of Aesthetics, The Oxford Companion to Western Art, and The Concise Oxford Dictionary of Art Terms.

name: Oxford Bibliographies
description: Literary guide to significant sources on a variety of subjects. Works as an annotated bibliography and a high-level encyclopedia.

name: Oxford Companion to American Law
description: Authoritative reference work on the subject of American law

name: Oxford Companion to Law
description: Fundamentals of all major areas of law: criminal, tax and social security, human rights, family and employment, education, sports, international and EU; the role and workings of legal institutions such as parliaments, courts, law schools, and international bodies such as the EU and the UN; leading cases, famous trials and distinguished lawyers; and major events in legal history and major debates in legal theory.

name: Oxford Companion to the Supreme Court of the United States
description: Entries on key cases and full treatment of crucial areas of constitutional law.

name: Oxford Constitutions of the World
description: Constitutions for every jurisdiction across the world including national, sub-national, and state constitutions, as well as those of special sovereignties and recognized autonomous regions.

name: Oxford Dictionary of National Biography
description: Deceased people who have shaped British history and culture, worldwide, from the Romans to the 21st century. Concise, up-to-date biographies written by named, specialist authors.

name: Oxford Encyclopaedic Dictionary of International Law
description: Cases, treaties, journal articles, and websites on international law including diplomatic law, criminal law, human rights, etc. concise but substantial information on: legal terms as used in international law, significant doctrines, prominent cases, decisions and arbitration, important incidents, judicial and literary figures, treaties and conventions, organizations and institutions, and acronyms.

name: Oxford Guide to United States Supreme Court Decisions
description: Entries on major Supreme Court cases written by eminent legal scholars. Each entry provides the United States Reports citation, the date the case was argued and decided, the vote of the Justices, who wrote the opinion for the Court, who concurred, who dissented, an informative account of the particulars of the case, the legal and social background, the reasoning behind the Courts decision, and the impact on American society.

name: Oxford Handbooks Online
description: Articles and books on Archaeology, Classical Studies, Criminology and Criminal Justice, History, Philosophy, Political Science, and Sociology. Some content in Business & Management, Economics & Finance, Literature, Psychology, and Religion.

name: Oxford Historical Treaties
description: Comprehensive collection of treaties of all nations concluded from 1648 through 1919. Full text of The Consolidated Treaty Series.

name: Oxford International Encyclopedia of Legal History
description: Legal history from ancient to modern times. Ancient Greek Law, Ancient Roman Law, Medieval Roman Law, Chinese Law, English Common Law, Islamic Law, United States Law, and the laws of such other regions as Africa, Latin America, and South Asia. Private law (contract, tort, civil procedure), public law (statutory, criminal, etc.), and higher or constitutional law.

name: Oxford Music Online
description: Includes: Grove Music Online, The Oxford Dictionary of Music, and the Oxford Companion to Music. These may be searched together or individually. Grove Music Online contains the following important music reference sources: The New Grove Dictionary of Music and Musicians, The New Grove Dictionary of Opera, and The New Grove Dictionary of Jazz.

meta.more_info: Vendor Tutorial: Grove Music Online User's Manual

name: Oxford Reference
description: Reference sources published by Oxford University Press. Dictionaries, subject reference works, language reference sources and key titles in the Oxford Companion series. Some examples are: the Oxford-Duden German dictionaries, A Dictionary of Psychology, A Dictionary of Zoology, The Oxford Dictionary of Idioms, A Dictionary of Writers and Their Works, Oxford Companion to Western Art, Oxford Companion to U.S.History, Oxford Companion to Music, Oxford Companion to Philosophy, and The Encyclopedia of Popular Music, etc.

name: Oxford Reference: Performing Arts
description: Performing arts reference sources: Oxford Companion to the American Musical, Oxford Companion to American Theatre, Oxford Dictionary of Dance, Oxford Encyclopedia of Latinos and Latinas in the United States, Grove Encyclopedia of Materials and Techniques in Art, Concise Oxford Dictionary of Music, Oxford Companion to Music, Dictionary of Opera Characters, New Grove Book of Opera Singers, Grove Book of Operas, Concise Oxford Dictionary of Opera, Encyclopedia of Popular Music, Oxford Companion to Shakespeare, Oxford Encyclopedia of Theatre and Performance, and the Concise Oxford Companion to the Theatre.

name: Oxford Reports on International Law
description: Public international case law on international courts, domestic courts and ad hoc tribunals. All reported decisions have a headnote, the full text of the decision, and are linked to the Oxford Law Citator.

name: Oxford Scholarly Authorities on International Law
description: Reference works and treatises on international law.

name: PAIS International
description: Public Affairs Information Service: journal articles, books, government documents, statistical directories, grey literature, research reports, conference papers, web content, etc. covering issues in the public debate.

meta.more_info: Coverage: PAIS International is the current file covering 1972 to present, and PAIS Archive includes the content from printed volumes published 1915-1976.

name: Papal Encyclicals Online
description: Encyclicals and other Vatican documents from Pope Honorious III (1216-1227) to the present. Many documents are in English, some are available in other languages (e.g., Latin or Italian), and some are available in multiple languages. Church council documents are also included.

name: PARES: Portal de Archivos Espaoles
description: Documents on Spain and its colonies in Spanish archives, including the Archivo General de Indias, the Archivo General de Simancas, and other historical archives. Includes images of the Spanish Civil War (Archivo rojo).

meta.more_info: Coverage: Historic through Spanish Civil War.

name: Past Masters
description: Scholarly editions of major philosophers, from Aristotle to Santayana; and the collected letters of major English writers: Austen, Burney, Coleridge, Wordsworth, and selected Romantics. Also includes the Oxford Classical Dictionary and the Oxford Duden German Dictionary.

name: Patrologia Latina
description: The first edition of Jacques-Paul Migne's Patrologia Latina, published between 1844 and 1855, and the four volumes of indexes published between 1862 and 1865. The Patrologia Latina comprises the works of the Church Fathers from Tertullian in 200 AD to the death of Pope Innocent III in 1216.

name: Patrologiae Graeca
description: Greek material (with Latin translations) relevant to the study of the history of the Christian Church from its beginnings through the Council of Florence in 1439. Texts are interlaced, with columns of Greek and Latin.

name: Periodicals Index Online
description: Article citations in the arts, humanities and social sciences. Covers 40 languages and dialects, including English, French, German, Italian, Spanish and other Western languages. Includes bibliographic citations, but no abstracts.

name: Philosopher's Index
description: Abstracts covering scholarly research in all areas of philosophy. Journal articles, books, book chapters, such as contributions to an anthology, and book reviews.

name: PTSDpubs
description: Published International Literature On Traumatic Stress, sponsored by the US Department of Veterans Affairs. Citations and abstracts to the international literature on post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) and other mental-health consequences of traumatic events.

name: Political Handbook of the World (CQ)
description: Major aspects of each country's government and political party system: key facts, government and political history, current issues, political parties and organizations, legislatures and cabinets, and communications. Profiles of international organizations.

name: Popular Medicine in America
description: Books, pamphlets, trade cards, and advertising ephemera showing popular remedies and treatments in nineteenth century America, aimed at the general public rather than medical professionals.

name: Post-Reformation Digital Library (PRDL)
description: Books relating to the development of theology and philosophy during the Reformation and Post-Reformation/Early Modern Era (late 15th-18th c.). Late medieval and patristic works printed and referenced in the early modern era also included.

name: Presidential Recordings Digital Edition
description: Recordings and annotated transcripts of telephone conversations of Presidents Kennedy, Johnson, and Nixon. Transcripts are searchable and browseable by administration, series, speaker, date, place, and duration.

name: Presidential Recordings Program
description: American presidential recordings in the White House between 1940 and 1973, presidential speeches, and presidential oral histories.

name: PRISMA: Publicaciones y Revistas Sociales y Humanisticas
description: Comprehensive reference resource of articles in the social sciences and humanities for the interdisciplinary academic study of Hispanic and Latin America and the Caribbean Basin. Key titles in English, Spanish and Portuguese.

name: ProceedingsFirst
description: Every published congress, symposium, conference, exposition, workshop and meeting received by The British Library Document Supply Centre.

name: Project MUSE
description: Digital humanities and social science scholarly journals from university presses and scholarly societies.

name: ProQuest
description: Search all ProQuest databases simultaneously. Includes arts, humanities, social sciences, news, and science and technology.

name: Proquest Congressional Publications
description: U.S. Congressional materials (hearings, bills, Congressional Record and compiled legislative histories) and Executive Branch documents.

name: ProQuest Criminal Justice
description: U.S. and international scholarly journals, it includes correctional and law enforcement trade publications, crime reports, crime blogs and other material relevant for researchers or those preparing for careers in criminal justice, law enforcement and related fields. A recommended resource for researching criminology, prisons and incarceration, policing, the criminal justice system.

name: ProQuest Recent Newspapers - Digital Microfilm
description: Digital replacement of microfilm for several major papers (Los Angeles Times, New York Times, Tennessean, Wall Street Journal, Washington Post).

meta.more_info: Access Note: You must click the "select" link to access the title.

name: ProQuest Historical Newspapers
description: Historical access to the following newspapers: Atlanta Constitution, Atlanta Daily World, The Baltimore Afro-American, The Boston Globe, The Chicago Defender, The Christian Science Monitor, Chicago Tribune, The Cincinnati Enquirer, Cleveland Call & Post, The Commercial Appeal, Detroit Free Press, The Globe and Mail, Hartford Courant, The Jerusalem Post, The Jewish Advocate, The Jewish Exponent, Korea Times, Knoxville News Sentinel, Leftist Newspapers, Los Angeles Sentinel, Los Angeles Times, Louisville Courier Journal, Michigan Chronicle, New York Amsterdam News, The New York Times, New York Tribune, Norfolk Journal & Guide, The Philadelphia Tribune, Pittsburgh Courier, San Francisco Chronicle, South China Morning Post, St. Louis Post Dispatch, The Tennessean, The Times of India, The Wall Street Journal, and The Washington Post.

name: ProQuest Legislative Insight
description: Publications created by the United States Congress during the process leading up to the enactment of U.S. Public Laws.

name: PsycARTICLES
description: APA PsycARTICLES is a full-text database for social and behavioral science journals published by the American Psychological Association, the APA Educational Publishing Foundation, the Canadian Psychological Association, and Hogrefe & Huber. The database includes all material from the print journals.

meta.more_info: Access Note: Note: all articles in PsycArticles are also indexed and linked from the PsycINFO database, which provides broader subject coverage.

name: Public Agenda
description: Reports, discussion starters, books, and multimedia resources on education, energy, health care, and the federal budget and national debt.

name: PubMed Central (PMC)
description: Freely available journal literature in the life sciences from the National Center for Biotechnology Information and the National Library of Medicine.

name: RAMBI: Index of Articles on Jewish Studies
description: Articles in Jewish studies from the National Library of Israel.

name: Readers' Guide Retrospective, 1890-1982
description: Index of articles from magazines, including full coverage of the original print volumes of Readers Guide to Periodical Literature. Atlantic Monthly, Century Magazine, Christian Century, Commonweal, Foreign Affairs, Fortnightly Review (London), Good Housekeeping, Harper's, Ladies Home Journal, Literary Digest, The Nation, New Republic, Reader's Digest, Saturday Evening Post, Saturday Review, Scribner's, and Women's Home Companion.

name: Reaxys
description: Chemical reactions, chemical substances and measured substance property data from chemistry journals and organic chemistry patent publications. Synthesis planner and the ability to generate a structure query from a name. Reaxys Xcelerate Direct enables fast insights into the data as well as instantaneous synthesis planning.

name: ProQuest Regulatory Insight
description: United States federal administrative law histories organized by federal statute and executive order.

name: Religion and Philosophy Collection
description: Journals on world religions, major denominations, biblical studies, religious history, epistemology, political philosophy, philosophy of language, moral philosophy and the history of philosophy.

name: Revised Common Lectionary
description: Ecumenical lectionary system developed by the North American Consultation on Common Texts (CCT) and the International English Language Liturgical Consultation (ELLC). Based on the Roman Lectionary, Ordo Lectionum Missae.

name: RILM Abstracts of Music Literature (Ebscohost)
description: International index of journal articles, books, conference proceedings, reviews, catalogs, and dissertations, primarily in the areas of musicology, ethnomusicology, music theory, and analysis.

name: RIM (Research in Ministry) Online
description: Index of DMin and DMiss projects from reporting schools of theology accredited by the Association of Theological Schools in the United States and Canada.

meta.more_info: Coverage: 1981 to present. Updated four times a year (on February 15, May 15, August 15, November 15).

name: RIPM - Retrospective Index to Music Periodicals (RIPM Platform)
description: International, highly annotated bibliography with detailed content analysis of writings on musical history and culture. Index of music periodicals including articles, reviews, illustrations, music examples, advertisements, press reviews, and more.

name: Routledge Encyclopedia of Philosophy
description: Scholarly articles on individual philosophers and concepts, and survey articles on major philosophical themes. Includes non-Western traditions.

name: Sabin Americana
description: Books, pamphlets, serials and other works about the Americas, through the early 1900s. Original accounts of exploration, trade, colonialism, slavery and abolition, the western movement, Native Americans, military actions, etc.

name: Schillers Werke im WWW
description: Complete works of Friedrich von Schiller: poetry, plays, translations, minor prose works, historical and philosophical writings, letters to and from Schiller, and the Conversations. German version and English version.

name: SciELO Scientific Electronic Library Online
description: Journals and books published in Brazil and other countries in Latin America on health care, sciences, social sciences, literature, and arts.

meta.more_info: Coverage: Open access for many journals available since the late 1990s.

name: Scribner Writers Series
description: Essays on authors and literary genres drawn from Scribner print series.

name: Scripta Sinica
description: Classic Chinese texts, especially those related to Chinese history. Part of the "Full-Text Chinese Records Database" (Han ji quan wen zi liao ku).

name: Scriptores Codicum Medii Aevi
description: List of manuscripts and biographical information manuscript collectors/owners. Single manuscripts are also searchable for scribe and ownership. Interface in German.

name: Searchable Greek Inscriptions
description: Collection of ancient Greek inscriptions. Can be used in tandem with PHI Workplace Version 10.

name: Senate Executive Journals, Series I, 1789-1866
description: United States Senate's record of its proceedings in executive session, capturing the minutes of floor action on military events, judicial appointments, foreign affairs and other important issues.

name: Shakespeare in Quarto
description: The British Library's quarto texts of Shakespeare's plays. Background information about Shakespeare, his works, the theatre world of his time, the printing of his plays, and a list of references and links to websites of special relevance to the quartos.

name: Shen Bao
description: Shanghai-based newspaper.

name: Short Story Index
description: Index of important collections and anthologies of short stories that are published each year. All literary genres are covered.

meta.more_info: Coverage: Index, 1984 to present; Full text, 1994 to present.

name: Si bu cong kan
description: Authoritative editions of the original and historical monographs published by the Commercial Press in 1919-1936. Holdings span Song, Yuan, Ming and Qing Dynasties including early manuscripts and rare prints (antiquarian, out-of-print books).

name: Slavery and Manumission Manuscripts of Timbuktu
description: 19th century manuscripts relating to slavery and manumission in Timbuktu, providing documentation of Africans in slavery in Muslim societies. In Arabic.

name: Slavery, Abolition & Social Justice
description: Primary source documents on key aspects of the history of slavery. The African Coast, the Middle Passage, the varieties of slave experience, religion, revolts, abolition, and legislation. Case studies from America, the Caribbean, Brazil, and Cuba.

name: Smithsonian Global Sound for Libraries
description: Encyclopedia of the world's musical and aural traditions. Contains music recordings.

name: Social Services Abstracts
description: Bibliographic coverage of current research focused on social work, human services and related areas, including social welfare, social policy and community development. Abstracts and index of serials publications, journal articles, dissertations, and citations to book reviews.

name: Sociological Abstracts
description: Index of international literature in sociology and related disciplines in the social and behavioral sciences. Abstracts of journal articles, books, book chapters, dissertations, conference papers, and citations to book reviews.

name: Something about the Author (SATA) (Gale)
description: Biographical information, essays, photos, and illustrations on well-known and emerging authors and illustrators.

name: SPIE Digital Library
description: Optics and photonics research. Covers applied optics and photonics science and engineering papers. Technology applications from imaging, sensors, biomedicine, manufacturing, energy, nanotechnology, communications, entertainment, and electronics.

name: SpringerProtocols
description: Molecular biology and biomedical protocols. Includes some video protocols.

name: State Papers Online: Early Modern Government in Britain and Europe
description: British state papers and government documents. SPO, 1509-1714 encompasses the 16th- and 17th-century State Papers Domestic, Foreign, Scotland, Ireland and Registers of the Privy Council from The National Archives, Kew, UK with some series from the British Library. SPO, Eighteenth Century, 1714-1782 covers the 18th century from the accession of the Hanoverian monarchs to the British throne in 1714 to the end of the State Papers series in 1782.

name: Sunday Times (London)
description: Full images of the pages of The Sunday Times (London).

name: Supreme Court Database
description: Information about each case decided by the Court. Identity of the court whose decision the Supreme Court reviewed, the parties to the suit, the legal provisions considered in the case, and the votes of the Justices.

name: SUR
description: SUR was "one of the most important and influential literary magazines published in Latin America in the 20th Century." This resource includes images of the complete magazine including covers, photographs, and advertisements as well as a comprehensive index. A set of images of manuscripts from the first issue and an unpublished set of letters by Victoria Ocampo are also included.

name: Synoptic Gospels Primer
description: Tools for the literary analysis of the Synoptic Gospels, including search, a glossary, and links to other resources on the issue of the relationship among the three gospel texts.

name: TaxNotes
description: Comprehensive, impartial coverage of tax news; commentary contributing to the understanding and application of tax policy.

meta.more_info: Access Note: You must create a login while on the Vanderbilt campus. Help Center: https://www.taxnotes.com/support/help-center

name: Teacher Reference Center
description: Indexing and abstracts for popular teacher and administrator journals and magazines to assist professional educators.

name: Teatro Espanol del Siglo de Oro
description: First editions of Spanish plays from the 16th and 17th centuries (e. g., Lope de Vega, Cervantes, Ruz de Alarcn) with text copied exactly as written, with all peculiarities captured (accents, abbreviations, etc.).

name: Tennessean (2002-present)
description: Nashville's local and regional newspaper: The Tennessean.

meta.more_info: Coverage: Nov. 7, 2002, to date, with incomplete coverage back to Jan. 1, 1999.

name: Tennessee Electronic Library (for residents of Tennessee only)
description: Research databases available to all Tennessee residents. Includes magazines, journals, newspapers, e-books, primary source materials, standardized test preparation, genealogy records, and career search help. NOTE: To access remotely (e.g., off campus or outside of a Tennessee library), you will need to enter a Tennessee public library card number.

meta.more_info: TEL is administered by the Tennessee State Library & Archives.

name: Test Link
description: Index of information on published and unpublished tests.

name: Theatre in Video
description: Streaming performances of the world's leading plays and film documentaries representing hundreds of leading playwrights, actors and directors. Gull text of plays, their history of performance, production background, reference materials and ephemera. Plays are predominately 20th century and Shakespeare productions.

name: Thesaurus Linguae Graecae (TLG)
description: Texts written in Greek from Homer to the fall of Byzantium. Personal user account required for search. Click "register" link to set up account. Select HELP links on the main database web page when unsure about input and Greek display.

meta.more_info: Access Note: Personal user account is required for search. Click "register" link in upper right if account is needed. Select the HELP links on the main database Web page if you are unsure which input and Greek display option you should choose. For assistance with using Thesaurus Linguae Graecae, please consult the online help.

name: Thesaurus Linguae Latinae (TLL)
description: Latin texts from the classical period up to about 600 C.E.

name: Times Digital Archive (London Times)
description: Full images of the pages of The Times (London) including advertising, book reviews, letters to the editor, and obituaries as well as news articles.

name: Times Literary Supplement Historical Archive (TLS)
description: Weekly literary journal founded in 1902 as a supplement to The Sunday Times of London. Provides comprehensive coverage of the latest and most important scholarly publications, in every subject, in several languages. Also reviews current fiction, nonfiction, theatre, cinema, music and exhibitions. Widely considered the finest literary review in the English language, TLS sets the tone and standards for excellence in the field of literary criticism. Many distinguished writers have been contributors, including T. S. Eliot, Henry James, and Virginia Woolf.

meta.more_info: Coverage: Begins 1902. Most recent 5 years not available.

name: TOCS-IN: Tables of Contents of Journals of Interest to Classicists
description: Tables of contents of classics, near eastern studies, and religion journals.

name: TRACfed
description: Comprehensive information about federal staffing, spending, and the enforcement activities of the federal government. Federal enforcement activities: staffing, funds, and the diverse characteristics of counties, federal districts, and states.

name: Trade Policy Reviews - World Trade Organization
description: Focuses on the World Trade Organization members' own trade policies and practices. They also take into account the countries' wider economic and developmental needs, their policies and objectives,and the external economic environment that they face.

name: Twayne's Authors Series
description: Literary criticism: introductions to the lives and works of writers, the history and influence of literary movements, and the development of literary genres

name: U. S. Congressional Serial Set (1789-2012) - ProQuest
description: Contains Documents and Reports from the U.S. Senate and House of Representatives. Includes committee reports related to bills and other matters, presidential communications to Congress, maps, treaty materials, as well as some non-governmental publications. During for much of the 19th century, especially in the pre-Civil War era, the Serial Set includes materials originating not only from the U.S. Congress but also key Executive Department publications.

name: U. S. Congressional Serial Set (1817-1979) - Readex
description: House and Senate Documents and the House and Senate Reports. For political, social, cultural, military and ethnic history, international relations, travel, commerce, industrial development, etc.

name: Ulrichsweb
description: ISSN, publisher, language, subject, abstracting & indexing coverage, full-text database coverage, tables of contents, and reviews written by librarians on periodicals of all types: academic and scholarly journals, e-journals, peer-reviewed titles, popular magazines, newspapers, newsletters, etc.

name: Union List of Artist Names
description: Biographical information and historical geographic data for artists and architects. Global coverage with an emphasis on Western European and American names. Includes artists and architects from the ancient period to contemporary times, with a post-medieval emphasis.

name: United Nations ECLAC Digital Repository (Economic Commission for Latin America and the Caribbean)
description: UN publications on: gender affairs; international trade and integration; economic development; production, poverty, and management; social development; sustainable development and human settlements; public administration and development planning; population and development; natural resources and infrastructure. In Spanish, English, and Portuguese.

name: United Nations Treaty Collection
description: All multilateral treaties deposited with the Secretary-General of the United Nations and those formerly deposited with the League of Nations. Bilateral and multilateral treaties registered with and published by the UN Secretariat. Detailed treaty references and full texts are available in all authentic languages.

name: United States Patent and Trademark Office
description: Conduct patent and trademark searches, download application forms, view images of US patents. Search the roster of patent agents and attorneys. Look up current fees and announcements.

name: Vanderbilt Television News Archive
description: The world's most extensive and complete archive of television news. All individual network evening news broadcasts since 1968 and special news-related programming since 1989.

name: Vanderbilt University Electronic Theses and Dissertations (ETD)
description: Vanderbilt University theses and dissertations.

name: Vetus Latina Database
description: Comprehensive patristic records for the Vetus Latina Institut in Beurum. Brepolis homepage in English, French, German, and Dutch; user interface in German; text in Latin.

meta.more_info: Access Note: Access is through the Brepolis web Gateway. Click on Enter Databases, then click on the GO button by Vetus Latina.

name: ViVa: A Bibliography of Women's History in Historical and Women's Studies Journals
description: Bibliography of women's and gender history in historical and women's studies journals. Articles in English, French, German, Dutch, Scandinavian languages and Spanish, are selected from European, American, Canadian, Asian, Australian and New Zealand journals.

name: Vogue Archive
description: Complete archive of American Vogue, documenting American and international fashion, culture, and society. High-resolution color page images, with every page, advertisement, cover, and fold-out included.

name: Voyages: The Trans-Atlantic Slave Trade Database
description: Records from slaving expeditions from archives and libraries throughout the Atlantic world. Information about vessels, enslaved peoples, slave traders and owners, and trading routes. Statistical tables, graphs, maps, and timelines.

name: Wabash Center Guide to Internet Resources for Teaching and Learning in Theology and Religion
description: Selective, annotated guide to resources of interest to those who are involved in the study and practice of religion: syllabi, electronic texts, electronic journals, websites, bibliographies, listserv discussion groups, liturgies, reference resources, software, etc.

name: Washington Post
description: First-hand accounts and unparalleled coverage of the politics, society and events of the time.

name: Waterloo Directory of English Newspapers and Periodicals: 1800-1900 (Series 2)
description: Bibliography of English publications, personal names, issuing bodies and subjects.

name: Web of Science Core Collection: Citation Indexes
description: Major journals across science disciplines. Complete bibliographic data and author abstracts. Each citation includes a list of references cited in the source article.

name: Web of Science: Arts & Humanities Citation Index (A&HCI)

name: Web of Science: Science Citation Index Expanded
description: Major journals across science disciplines. Complete bibliographic data and author abstracts. Each citation includes a list of references cited in the source article.

name: Web of Science: Social Sciences Citation Index (SSCI)
description: Social sciences journals, and science and technical journals related to the social sciences. Complete bibliographic data, author abstracts, and references cited.

name: Westlaw
description: Legal research system for Vanderbilt Law School faculty, students and staff. Please see a reference librarian for assistance.

meta.more_info: Please see a reference librarian for assistance.

name: Witchcraft Collection

Manifestations of the belief in witchcraft, its geographical boundaries, works on canon law, the Inquisition, torture, demonology, trial testimony, and narratives. Focuses on witchcraft not as folklore or anthropology, but as theology and as religious heresy.

name: Women and Social Movements in the United States
description: Document projects and archives, including book, film, and website reviews, notes from the archives, and teaching tools; organized around the history of women in social movements.

name: Women Working, 1800-1930
description: Digitized books, manuscripts, and images from the collections of Harvard University Libraries and Museums on the topic of women in the U. S. economy from 1800-1930.

name: Women Writers Online
description: Early womens writing, including full transcriptions of texts published between 1526 and 1850, focusing on materials that are rare or inaccessible.

name: Women's Magazine Archive
description: Womens interest consumer magazines with detailed article-level indexing. Includes Better Homes and Gardens, Chatelaine, Cosmopolitan, Essence, Good Housekeeping, Ladies' Home Journal, Parents, Redbook, Seventeen, WIN News, and Woman's Day.

name: World Bank Documents & Reports
description: Board documents, country focus, economic & sector work, project documents, publications & research.

name: World News Connection
description: Archive of foreign news translated into English. Newspaper articles, television and radio broadcasts, online sources, conference proceedings, periodicals, and non-classified reports.

name: World News Digest
description: Archival record of domestic and international news covering all major political, social, and economic events since November 1940. Facts on File.

name: World Newspaper Archive
description: Historical newspapers from North America, Latin America, the Caribbean, Africa and South Asia. (note: North American section includes Mexico, the Caribbean, Central America, and the US.)

meta.more_info: Access Note: Vanderbilt and CRL member institutions. Coverage: 18th - 20th centuries.

name: World Politics Review
description: Articles on foreign policy, international politics and foreign affairs by experts from journalism, research organizations, academia, government, etc.

name: Archives of Latin American and Caribbean History, Sixteenth to Twentieth Century
description: Primary source documents about Latin America and the Caribbean covering current issues and events as well as historical events back to the colonial period.

name: World Shakespeare Bibliography Online
description: Annotated entries for scholarly and popular Shakespeare materials. Reviews of books, productions, films, and audio recordings.

name: World Who's Who
description: Lives and achievements of the most distinguished men and women from almost every country in the world. Career, contact and other biographical details.

name: Worldwide Political Science Abstracts (WPSA)
description: Citations, abstracts, and indexing of the international serials literature in political science international relations, law, and public administration / policy. Includes merged backfiles of Political Science Abstracts and ABC POL SCI.

name: zbMATH

name: Rock's Backpages

name: Intelligize

name: AccessMedicine

name: ClinicalKey for Nursing

name: FAO Document Repository

Search or browse to identify individuals, departments and laboratories that have made fundamental contributions to the advancement of science and technology.

Education guides (Powerpoint slides), glossary, calculators, worksheets (critical appraisal guides and information cycle problem-solving aids), question wizards (to create and edit clinical questions), and podcasts. Create a profile to store favorite and most-used items for future use. Mobile version available.

name: Latino Literature: Poetry, Drama, and Fiction

name: East India Company

name: Indigenous Peoples: North America
description: Manuscripts, book collections, newspapers from various tribe and Indian-related organizations; materials such as Bibles, dictionaries and primers in Indigenous languages.

name: National Geographic
description: Archive of National Geographic magazine, and collection of National Geographic books, maps, images and videos.

name: Naxos Music Library World
description: Streaming audio library of world music. Covers Africa, the Americas, Asia, Europe, and Oceania.

name: Naxos Video Library
description: Streaming video library of classical music performances, opera, ballet, live concerts, and documentaries.

name: Nineteenth Century Collections Online
description: Monographs, newspapers, pamphlets, manuscripts, ephemera, maps, statistics, and more. Collections: British Politics and Society, Asia & The West, British Theatre and Popular Culture, Corvey Collection of European Literature, Europe and Africa, Photography, Science, Technology, and Medicine Parts 1 & 2, Women Transnational Networks, Children Literature and Childhood, Mapping the World, Religion and the Periodical.

name: Slavery and Anti-Slavery
description: Books and pamphlets, US Supreme Court records, newspapers and periodicals, manuscripts, and reference materials for the scholarly study and understanding of slavery from a multinational perspective.

name: Smithsonian Collections Online
description: Archival materials on topics such as the evolving modes of flight, World's Fairs, and trade literature. Smithsonian Magazine and Air & Space Magazine.

name: Women's Wear Daily Archive
description: Comprehensive archive of Womens Wear Daily, a fashion industry trade journal.

name: Women's Studies Archive
description: Diaries, letters, photographs, news clippings, organizational records, and journals. History of the social, political, and professional aspects of women's lives and the roles, experiences, and achievements of women in society.

description: Newly published Greek inscriptions and studies on previously known documents. Complete Greek texts of all new inscriptions, summary of new readings, interpretations, and studies of known inscriptions.

description: Humanities and social sciences periodicals. Includes Foundation Collection. Sister database to Periodicals Index Online.
