Other films may require a request through the Kanopy site. Viewing films in a group forum is permitted as long as the viewing is by Vanderbilt authorized viewers only (current faculty, staff, or students) and it is not for commercial benefit (i.e. no admission costs are charged and no profit is made from the screening).

name: KCI-Korean Journal Database
description: Articles from multidisciplinary journals covered in KCI. Managed by the National Research Foundation of Korea; contains bibliographic information for scholarly literature published in Korea.

name: Klapp Online- Bibliography of French Literature
description: Bibliography of French literature with annual updates.

name: Leisure, Travel and Mass Culture: The History of Mass Tourism
description: Guidebooks and brochures, periodicals, travel agency correspondence, photographs, and personal travel journals provide unique insight into the expansion, accessibility, and affordability of tourism for the masses and the evolution of some of the most successful travel agencies in the world.

name: Literary Manuscripts: 17th and 18th Century Poetry from the Brotherton Library, University of Leeds
description: Facsimile images of manuscripts of 17th and 18th century verse held in the Brotherton Library at the University of Leeds. Alongside original compositions are copied verses, translations, songs, and riddles.

name: Literary Manuscripts: Victorian Manuscripts from the Berg Collection of the New York Public Library
description: Victorian Manuscripts from the Henry W. and Albert A. Berg Collection of The New York Public Library. Contains unpublished poems, working notebooks, holograph manuscripts, and drawings which trace the inspiration and genesis behind the periods greatest works.

name: Literary Print Culture: The Stationers' Company Archive
description: Primary sources for studying the early book trade, the printing and publishing community, the establishment of legal requirements for copyright, and the history of bookbinding.

name: Macmillan Cabinet Papers
description: Cabinet conclusions (minutes) and memoranda of Harold Macmillans government, plus selected minutes and memoranda of policy committees. Casts new light on Britain's relationship with the EEC, Anglo-American ties, the Cold War, Decolonization, and issues of Public and Political Morality.

name: Making of Modern Law: American Civil Liberties Papers
description: ACLU newspaper clippings, scrapbooks, court files, memorandums, telegrams, minutes, and legal case records on free speech, citizenship, race, discrimination, immigration, labor, radicalism, etc. to support the study of American legal history.

name: Market Research and American Business Reports
description: Market research reports by consumer analyst Ernest Dichter and his Institute for Motivational Research, commissioned by advertising agencies and businesses such as Philip Morris, Chrysler, and CBS on products ranging from tobacco and broadcasting to cars and hotels.

name: Mass Observation Online
description: Manuscripts from the Mass Observation project which studied the lives of ordinary people in Britain from 1937 into the early 1960s. Provides valuable information about Britain before, during, and after World War II, and about changes in attitudes to many topics in social and economic life.

name: MathSciNet: Mathematical Reviews (EBSCOhost)
description: Pure and applied mathematics and statistics, and applications to other fields, such as physics, computer science, and engineering. Most entries contain an author's abstract or critical review.

name: Medical Services and Warfare
description: Explore multiple perspectives on the history of injury, treatment, and disease on the front line. Chart scientific advances through hospital records, medical reports, and first-hand accounts, and discover the evidence of how war shaped medical practice across the centuries.

name: Medieval Family Life
description: Letter correspondence of five 15th century English families describing everyday life including discussions of advantageous marriages and inheritance, estate management, women and their role within the family, arguments between parents and children, births, deaths, politics, and community.

name: Nixon Years
description: Complete UK Foreign and Commonwealth Office (FCO) 7 and 82 files for the entire period of Richard Nixon's presidency.

name: Patrologia Orientalis
description: Corpus of patristic texts from the Christian East published in the Patrologia Orientalis series.

meta.more_info: Access Note: Terms and Conditions: http://www.brepolis.net/terms.html.

name: ProQuest Research Library
description: Multidisciplinary scholarly journals, professional and trade publications, and general interest magazines.

name: ProQuest Supreme Court Insight
description: Complete collection of full opinions from U.S. Supreme Court argued cases, plus dockets, oral arguments, joint appendices, and amicus briefs

name: Race Relations in America
description: Speeches, reports, surveys, and analyses produced by the Race Relations Department of the United Church Board for Homeland Ministries based at Fisk University.

name: Russian Science Citation Index
description: Bibliographic information and citations to scholarly articles from Russian researchers in science, technology, medical, and education journals. Publications curated by the Scientific Electronic Library (eLIBRARY.RU), Russia's largest research information provider.

name: SciELO Scientific Electronic Library Online (Web of Science)
description: Scholarly literature in sciences, social sciences, arts and humanities published in leading open access journals from Latin American, Portugal, Spain and South Africa.

name: Scientific American Surgery
description: General surgical reference. Includes drawings, graphs, photos, and decision-making algorithms.

name: Service Newspapers of World War Two
description: Wartime newspapers published for soldiers serving in major theatres around the world. Both Allied and Axis publications are presented, offering a broad view of the war and the experiences of those on its front lines.

name: Socialism on Film
description: This collection of films from the communist world reveals war, history, current affairs, culture and society as seen through the socialist lens. It spans most of the twentieth century and covers countries such as the USSR, Vietnam, China, Korea, much of Eastern Europe, the GDR, Britain and Cuba.

name: UpToDate
description: Clinical resource, mostly in internal medicine. E-textbooks; information on drugs/drug interactions; images and videos; medical calculators and decision tools, "practice changing" current information, and training resources. Searchable in Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, English, French, German, Italian, Japanese, Portuguese, and Spanish. Users must create a free personal account to access from off-campus .

name: Visual History Archive (USC Shoah Foundation)
description: Streaming video collection of primary source testimonies of survivors and witnesses of crimes against humanity.

name: Women in The National Archives
description: Finding aid to womens studies resources in The National Archives (UK) and original documents covering the campaign for women's suffrage in Britain and the granting of women's suffrage in colonial territories.

name: Zoological Record
description: Worlds leading taxonomic reference and oldest continuing database of animal biology.

name: Taiwan Journals Search =
description: Academic journals from the humanities and social sciences published in Taiwan. New titles continue to be added.

name: Late Qing dynasty periodical full-text database
description: Journals about the Late Qing dynasty

name: Chinese Periodical Full-Text Database
description: Chinese periodicals published in the Republican era.

name: InfoEd Global SPIN
description: Searchable database of over 40,000 funding opportunities from more than 10,000 global sponsors. See INSTRUCTIONS for creating an account.

name: American Indian Newspapers
description: National periodicals as well as local community news and student publications covering subjects from an Indigenous perspective, including the civil rights era and American Indian Movement (AIM), education, environmentalism, land rights, and cultural representation.

name: Tennessean (1812-2002)
description: Full-text archive of Nashville's main daily newspaper.

name: Overdrive eBooks, Audiobooks, & Magazines
description: Access fiction and nonfiction e-books and audiobooks as well as magazines on a computer or through the Libby app on a mobile device.

name: Medici.tv
description: Classical music channel offering broadcasts of concerts, operas, ballets, documentaries, and master classes.

name: RILM Music Encyclopedias
description: Reference works covering current and historical musicology and ethnomusicology with subject-specific focus from popular music, opera, instruments, blues and gospel, to recorded music and women composers.

name: Database of Chinese Popular Literature =
description: Popular literary works from the Qin-Han Dynasties to the early years of the Republic of China, including novel, script for story-telling, theatrical text, story song, storytelling in southern dialects, common saying story, rare books, and treasure volume.

name: HistoryMakers Digital Archive
description: Oral history interviews with both well-known and unsung African American artists, business leaders, scientists, athletes, and intellectuals.

name: LGBT Magazine Archive
description: Archival runs of 26 of the most influential, longest-running serial publications covering more than six decades of the history and culture of the LGBT community.

name: News, Policy, & Politics Magazine Archive
description: Archive of 15 major magazines (including Newsweek), spanning areas including history of politics, current events, international relations, and public policy.

name: Religious Magazine Archive
description: Archive of magazines covering a wide variety of denominations and religious movements, allowing for comparative studies of religions during this period.

name: PitchBook
description: Private equity and venture capital database with in-depth information and statistics on a wide range of companies including startups. Establish benchmarks, find real time data on deals, identify and analyze comparables, and search investor details. Users are limited to 10 downloads per day and 25 downloads per month. Licensed for academic & curricular uses only.

name: American Historical Periodicals from the American Antiquarian Society Series 1-5
description: Documentary history of the American experience spanning four centuries with multiple perspectives on the thought, culture, and society of North America. These periodicals present history through the eyes of those who experienced it, showing its impact on citizens from all walks of life.

name: Cuban Culture and Cultural Relations, 1959-, Part 1: Casa y Cultura
description: Primary source materials from the Archivo Vertical at Casa de las Amricas library document the activities of the institution both in Cuba and beyond, bearing testimony to the conflicts and passions of a turbulent time.

name: Understanding Hate in America : White Nationalism and the Press in the 1920s
description: Local, regional, and national newspapers published by Klan organizations and by sympathetic publishers from across the U.S. Also includes key anti-Klan voices from newspapers published by ethnic, Catholic, and Jewish organizations.

name: Bloomsbury Popular Music
description: Explores the historical origins and cultural impact of popular music, influential artists and albums, local music scenes and subcultures, musical form, instruments, the workings of the music industry, and the social, political, and economic context of different musical genres.

name: Academic Video Online
description: Documentaries, interviews, performances, news programs and newsreels, field recordings, commercials, raw footage, and thousands of award-winning films .

name: Black Thought and Culture
description: Collection of non-fiction writings of leading African-Americans. Includes interviews, journal articles, speeches, essays, pamplets, letters and other fugitive material.

name: LGBT Thought and Culture
description: Books, periodicals, and archival materials documenting LGBT political, social, and cultural movements.

name: America in World War Two: Oral Histories and Personal Accounts
description: Oral histories, correspondence, diaries, photographs, artifacts, and military records of American military personnel and civilians show how World War Two changed American society and the economy, how it impacted individuals and their families, and the legacy of the war in human terms.

name: Shakespeare's Globe Archive: Theatres, Players & Performance
description: Documents the performance and construction history of the new Globe and the indoor theater space of the Sam Wanamaker Playhouse through prompt books, wardrobe notes, programs, publicity material, annual reports, show reports, photographs and architectural plans.

name: Black Abolitionist Papers
description: Primary sources from African Americans actively involved in the movement to end slavery in the United States with some coverage of the abolitionist movement in England, Scotland, Ireland, and Canada,

name: Docuseek
description: Streaming access to documentary and social issue films from a variety of distributors, including Bullfrog, Collective Eye, and Icarus Films.

name: MLA Directory of Periodicals
description: Information about the journals and book series indexed within the MLA International Bibliography. Editorial addresses, phone numbers, fax numbers, e-mail addresses, frequency of publication, descriptions of scope, circulation figures, subscription prices and addresses, advertising information, links to online content, peer review, and submission guidelines.

name: Ethnomusicology: Global Field Recordings
description: Audio recordings, videos, field notebooks, and journals document musical traditions and how music interacts with different societies and cultures all over the globe.

meta.more_info: Coverage: Mid-20th century to the early 21st century.

name: EBSCOhost
description: Search all databases available through EBSCO.

name: Public Health Archives
description: Documents the evolution and impact of the twentieth-century public health system in the United States through correspondence, reports, pamphlets, ephemera, and more.

name: Capital IQ (Owen and Law)
description: Available for members of the Owen and Law community. Financial data for public & private companies worldwide; private capital firms; M&A transactions. Financial analysis tools to generate reports: comparable analysis, market analysis, financial modeling, etc. First-time users must register for a personal account using their vanderbilt.edu email address.

name: Sex & Sexuality
description: Correspondence, research papers, and records from the Kinsey Institute Library and Special Collections explores changing attitudes towards human sexuality, gender identities, and sexual behaviors.

name: O'Reilly for Higher Education
description: Instruction and reference materials on computer programming, technology, and business transformation. The platform includes books, videos, case studies, audio books, and structured learning paths. An O'Reilly user account will automatically be created for first time users. For details, please refer to the O'Reilly Privacy Policy . Mobile app is available at https://www.oreilly.com/online-learning/apps.html .

name: Sanford Guide
description: Evidence-based clinical reference guide for the diagnosis and treatment of infectious diseases.

name: Ebook Central Alumni Access
description: Ebooks from publishers in all academic subject areas. Alumni login using their VUNetID .

name: ChemRxiv: The Preprint Server for Chemistry
description: ChemRxiv is a free submission, distribution and archive service for unpublished preprints in chemistry and related areas. It gives researchers across a broad range of chemistry fields the opportunity to share early results with colleagues and respond to comments and recommendations for improvement, ahead of formal peer review and publication.

name: Arxiv: The Preprint Server for Physics and More
description: Archive of scholarly journals in the fields of physics, mathematics, computer science, quantitative biology, quantitative finance, statistics, electrical engineering and systems science, and economics.

name: BioRxiv: The Preprint Server for Biology
description: bioRxiv is a free online archive and distribution service for unpublished preprints in the life sciences. It is operated by Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory, a not-for-profit research and educational institution. By posting preprints on bioRxiv, authors are able to make their findings immediately available to the scientific community and receive feedback on draft manuscripts before they are submitted to journals.

name: MedRxiv : Preprint Server for the Health Sciences
description: medRxiv is a free online archive and distribution server for complete but unpublished manuscripts (preprints) in the medical, clinical, and related health sciences.

name: TechRxiv : Preprints in Technology Research
description: TechRxiv is an open, moderated preprint server for unpublished research in electrical engineering, computer science, and related technology. By using TechRxiv, authors can quickly disseminate their work to a wide audience and gain community feedback on a draft version of their research. A preprint is a draft version of an article; final versions of published articles should not be submitted to TechRxiv.

name: EngRxiv : The open archive of engineering
description: engrXiv (engineering archive) is dedicated to the dissemination of engineering knowledge quickly and efficiently. engrXiv was launched in beta in July 2016 under the direction of Devin R. Berg, associate professor of mechanical engineering at the University of Wisconsin-Stout, and in partnership with the Center for Open Science.

name: Music Online: Classical Scores Library
description: Full, study, piano, and vocal scores encompass all major classical musical genres and time periods from the Middle Ages to the 21st century.

name: AccessEngineering
description: Reference and teaching platform that delivers interdisciplinary engineering ebooks and videos integrated with analytical teaching and learning tools.

name: RKMA Market Research Reports
description: Guides for researching consumer markets, including market assessments and forecasts, sector trends, profiles of market leaders, and statistics.

name: Food and Drink in History
description: Key food and drink history stories from the evolution of food within everyday life to haute cuisine, charting key issues around agriculture and food production, and looking into advertising histories of key food and drink brands. The materials in this collection illustrate the deep links between food and identity, politics, power, gender, race, and socio-economic status.

name: Making of Modern Law: Landmark Records and Briefs of the U.S. Courts of Appeals, 1950-1980
description: Over half a million pages of briefs from appellants, appellees, and supporters (amicus briefs), with their respective replies, as well as appendices, memoranda, petitions, plaintiff statements, transcripts, and more from the various circuits of the U.S. Courts of Appeals.

name: Art in the Christian Tradition
description: Images representing the continuum of the practice of Christianity from the 1st century A.D to the present.

meta.more_info: Coverage: 1st century A.D. to the present.

name: OpenDOAR
description: Directory of repositories that provide free, open access to academic outputs and resources.

name: Recent Researches in Music Online
description: Contains scores and partbooks published in Recent Researches in Music series for Medieval, Renaissance, Baroque, Classical, 19th/20th centuries, American (including MUSA) and Oral Traditions.

name: Fortunoff Video Archive for Holocaust Testimonies
description: Collection of interviews with Holocaust survivors and witnesses that detail a wide range of experiences, including imprisonment in concentration camps, living in hiding, fighting with partisans or the resistance, rescuing and protecting those targeted by Nazi persecution, and escape to Allied or neutral countries. First time users must register for an account then search for and request access to an initial testimony. You only need to request access to one testimony to obtain viewing rights for the entire collection. Users must always be logged in to the site to view videos. Detailed instructions for access can be found at http://fortunoff.library.yale.edu/archive/search . Off-campus access is not working at this time.

name: Gallup Analytics
description: US and global polling data, new and historical, at the city, state, and national level. Microdata is available upon request. Please visit this resource guide to learn more about available data and to request access.

name: Leopard Solutions (Law users only)
description: Legal industry intelligence database provides specially curated legal market and law firm reports that provide an overview of the industry and job search feature highlighting attorney job opportunities. First time users must register for a personal account with their @vanderbilt.edu email.

name: Sinica Sinoweb Archive  (SSW)
description: Sinological periodicals published in Taiwan (mostly from the Academia Sinica).

name: CNKI China Doctoral/Master Dissertations Full-text Database
description: Dissertations and theses from major universities and institutions in mainland China.

name: Oxford Research Encyclopedias
description: Long-form overview articles written, peer-reviewed, and edited by leading scholars. Both foundational and cutting-edge topics are covered in order to develop, over time, an anchoring knowledge base for major areas of research. Access to selected content only. Some articles may not be available.

name: Wiley Digital Archive
description: Unique or rare historical primary sources, digitized from leading societies, libraries, and archives around the world, and made accessible in ways that tie directly to research outcomes and educational goals. Contains tools for searching, browsing, analyzing and visualizing primary source content.

meta.more_info: Vendor Tutorial: Wiley Digital Archives Training Hub

name: Persian E-Books Miras Maktoob
description: Works in both Persian and Arabic on Islamic history and culture.

name: Digital Theatre Plus
description: Streaming video collection of current, leading British theatre productions. Includes behind-the-scenes documentaries as well as teaching and learning resources to facilitate a deeper understanding of the productions and texts.

name: Harvard Business Review Press Collection
description: HBRs complete catalog of ebooks on a range of business subjects, including business communication, career development, entrepreneurship, finance, human resources & personnel management, marketing, organizational behavior, and strategic planning.

name: Trends & Policy: U.S. Immigration
description: Connects policies implemented by the U.S. government with the data-driven results and trends of those policies. Provides context with analytical reports, news articles, timelines, topic pages, and data visualization tools.

name: Special Ed Connection
description: Emphasizes school and childhood disability law with information on legal and policy updates and news, advocacy information, working with autism spectrum disorders, IDEA (Individuals with Disability Education Act), FERPA (Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act) and ADA (Americans with Disabilities Act). Access is via username and password. Please contact the Law Library for credentials.

name: Children's Literature and Culture
description: Digitized rare books, games, ephemera, and artwork produced for, about and, in some cases, by children.

name: Early Modern England: Society, Culture & Everyday Life, 1500-1700
Mass Observation Project
description: Narrative responses from approximately 1,300 informants document the social history of Britain. Questionnaires explored topics of current interest during the 1980s and 1990s including popular attitudes about the events in the Royal family's marriage and divorce - as well as on everyday life, family, the home, leisure, and food.

name: LSEG Workspace
description: Source of financial and economic data, news, and information. Provides access to analyst reports and select SDC data from the M&A section. Users must register for a personal account , which will be reset each semester. It is available to authorized users from Vanderbilt University for non-commercial, academic research. View, download, and printing limit for analyst reports and pdf documents of 150 pages per day, reset daily at midnight local time. Download limits for SDC data of 199 rows of excel data per day. Download limits for other types of data may apply.

name: Grant Forward
description: Provides information on federal and private grant funding opportunities for all disciplines and project types. Search by criteria such as sponsor, deadline date, and keyword. First time users must register for a personal account using your Vanderbilt email address at https://www.grantforward.com/signup . Create a researcher profile to receive personalized grant recommendations that match your research needs.

name: Early Modern England: Society, Culture & Everyday Life, 1500-1700
description: Primary sources that document how prominent families to 'ordinary' people experienced everyday working, family, religious, and administrative life across societies and regions in England.

name: Foreign Office files for South East Asia, 1963-1980
description: Primary source documents that offer insight into the political and economic challenges faced during this period as the region moved towards industrialization and establishing the foundations for economic growth.

name: Poverty, Philanthropy and Social Conditions in Victorian Britain
description: Discover what life was like for the poorest communities in Victorian Britain, and explore the government policy, social reform movements, and philanthropic efforts of charitable institutions that sought to alleviate poverty.

name: LGBTQ+ Source
description: Scholarly and popular LGBTQ+ publications in full text, plus historically important primary sources, including monographs, magazines, and newspapers. It also includes a specialized LGBTQ+ thesaurus containing thousands of terms.

name: Nineteenth Century Literary Society: the John Murray Publishing Archive
description: Primary source documents from the archive of the historic John Murray literary publishing company. Author correspondence, advertisements, financial papers, and drafts of seminal works document the golden era of the House of Murray from its inception in 1768.

name: Vault (Law)
description: Industry and career guides, including employer profiles; advice on consulting case interviews, financial interviews, and career trends. Message boards. First-time users must register for a personal account using their vanderbilt.edu email address.

name: RIPM Jazz Periodicals
description: Cover-to-cover scans of jazz journals and magazines published in the United States.

name: RIPM European and North American Music Periodicals
description: Full-text music periodicals from Europe and North America focused on the musical life in numerous world capitals.

name: AAMI Standards
description: Information on standards for the medical device industry.

meta.more_info: First-time users are required to create an individual account. Click on the "Register" link in the top right corner.

name: Stahl Online
description: Entire current portfolio of neuropsychopharmacology books by Dr. Stephen Stahl. Also includes access to illustrated guides, case studies, handbooks, and a psychopharmacology drug tool.

name: Almanac of the Federal Judiciary
description: Judicial profiles of every federal judge and key bankruptcy judges and magistrate judges. Profiles are based on interviews with lawyers who have argued cases before the federal judiciary. Entries cover judge's academic and professional background, experience on the bench, noteworthy rulings, and media coverage, and commentary by lawyers. Also includes helpful tips for shaping case strategy, insights into each judge's style, demeanor, knowledge, and management of courtroom proceedings.

name: Sage Business Cases
description: Global case studies allow users to examine real-life business decisions and strategies in order to inform their own research, best practices, and professional development. Instructors interested in accessing the teaching notes should email the Walker Management Library at managementlibrary@vanderbilt.edu .

name: Tabloids = Xiao Bao +
description: Smaller format newspapers published mostly in Shanghai recorded what broadsheets missed, emphasizing leisure and entertainment, including novels, essays, and trends in film, theater, dance, etc. Numerous talented scholars and tabloid writers contributed, and together they shaped the collective conception of popular culture and modern cities.

name: North-China Daily News & Herald Newspapers and Hong Lists =
description: English and Chinese versions of The North-China Herald, The North-China Daily News, The Chinese Shipping List & Advertiser, Hu Bao, Han Bao, Xiao Xian Bao, and The North-China Desk Hong List.

name: SAGE Research Methods
description: Tools for every step of designing and conducting a research project such as writing a research question, gathering and analyzing data, preparing a literature review, working with teams, and publishing findings.

name: Rotunda: The University of Virginia Press Digital Collections
description: Primary and secondary sources and collections in American history and culture. Includes access to:  Adams Papers  Diaries of Gouverneur Morris  Documentary History of the First Federal Congress  Documentary History of the Ratification of the Constitution  Dolley Madison  Papers of Alexander Hamilton  Papers of Andrew Jackson  Papers of Andrew Johnson  Papers of Benjamin Rush  Papers of Booker T. Washington  Papers of Daniel Webster  Papers of Eliza Lucas Pinckney and Harriott Pinckney Horry Digital Edition  Papers of George Washington  Papers of James K. Polk  Papers of James Madison  Papers of James Monroe  Papers of John Marshall  Papers of the Revolutionary Era Pinckney Statesmen  Papers of Thomas Jefferson  Papers of Ulysses S. Grant  Papers of Woodrow Wilson  People of the Founding Era  Selected Papers of John Jay

name: Newspapers.com Library Edition - Southeast Collection
description: Historical newspapers archive containing thousands of regional, state, and small local newspapers from the southeastern U.S.

name: Early American Imprints, Series II: Shaw-Shoemaker, 1801-1819
description: American books, pamphlets and broadsides published in the early part of the 19th century which are many of the rarest materials published in early America. Based on the noted "American Bibliography, 1801-1819" by Ralph R. Shaw and Richard H. Shoemaker.

name: Aspen Learning Library
description: Study aids from the following series: Examples & Explanations, Emanuel Law Outlines, Emanuel CrunchTime, Glannon Guides, Casenote Legal Briefs, Academic Success, Inside Series, Friedmans Practice Series, and more.

name: Trends & Policy: U.S. Healthcare
description: Connects various healthcare policies to their trending results. Timelines put the history of healthcare policy in chronological context and topic pages explain complex policy issues. Includes healthcare related public laws and hearings, legislative and executive branch reports, news articles and data series.

name: Lexis Digital Library (Law users only)
description: Legal ebooks that can be accessed on mobile devices and desktop platforms. Includes casebooks, study guides, treatises, practice guides, and more.

name: Hispanic American Newspapers, 1808-1980
description: Fully searchable newspapers from Latinx presses on both Hispanic American and Latin American subjects. Published in Spanish, English, and bilingually from across 22 states, the newspapers provide a diversity of unabridged voices, ranging from intellectuals and literary notables to politicians, union organizers, and grassroots figures.

name: CEPR Discussion Papers
description: Initial research papers from research fellows and affiliates of the Centre for Economic Policy and Research.

name: Embase
description: Citations and abstracts to international journal literature and conference abstracts covering biomedicine and worldwide drug literature focused on the pharmaceutical industry: effects, use, and administration of all current, potential and experimental drugs, side effects, manufacturers, and trade names of approved and prospective drugs. This database also includes journals not covered by MEDLINE.

name: Zhongguo ji ben gu ji ku
description: Also known as Database of Chinese Classic Ancient Books : includes pre-20th century Chinese books covering all fields of knowledge from the pre-Qin dynasty to the Republican period. Allows a side-by-side comparison between the transcriptions and original page images

name: AccessAnesthesiology
description: Key anesthesiology textbooks, guidelines, procedural videos, case studies, and review questions.

name: AccessSurgery
description: Key surgery textbooks, guidelines, procedural videos, images, case studies, and 3,000 ABSITE-style review questions.

name: Global Health
description: Provides abstract and citation information for public health research and practice and international medical and health research. Covers literature on developing countries, sociological aspects, and a wide range of biomedical topics.

name: MedOne Radiology
description: Radiology textbooks, images, videos, case studies, and review questions. Also includes the Radiology Board Review. Create a personal account to track your progress on the review questions.

name: MedOne ComSci
description: Audiology and speech language pathology textbooks, images, videos, case studies, and review questions. Create a personal account to track your progress on the review questions.

name: Journalytics Medicine
description: Provides contact and publication information, quality metrics, submission experience data, and peer-review indicators for thousands of verified medical journals. Also includes list of predatory journals with criteria for inclusion and severity of violations.

name: Statista Alumni Access
description: Quantitative data, statistics, infographics, and related information compiled from market research institutes, trade publications, scientific journals, and government databases. Please note: alumni access does not include Market Insights, Consumer Insights, Company Insights, or eCommerce Insights.

name: Black South African Magazines
description: Digital collection of rare and historically significant magazines written for Black audiences in Africa from 1937 to 1973.

name: Global Think Tanks
description: Curated, high quality policy reports, briefs, analyses, working papers, and datasets produced by the worlds leading policy experts, think tanks, research centers, IGOs, and NGOs.

name: North American City Reports
description: Surveys, budgets, statistical records, case studies, planning documents, training manuals, policy guidelines, reports, and news from the worlds major cities. Provides research with particular importance in areas of climate change, political instability, and rapid growth.

name: History and Culture (Africa Commons)
description: African materials in books, magazines, newspapers, historical journals, government documents, oral history, photographs, art, music, and videos sourced from African organizations and archives around the world.

name: Southern African Films and Documentaries
description: Propaganda films, documentaries, newsreels, and interviews covering African history, politics, and culture. Films show township culture before apartheid, life under apartheid, and major historical events as they unfolded.

name: Decolonization: Politics and Independence in Former Colonial and Commonwealth Territories
description: Pamphlets, reports, speech transcripts, and other materials related to the transition to self-rule in different nations, and the ways in which former colonies and Commonwealth nations negotiated their own agency and their own futures.

name: Music Online: The Qwest TV Collection
description: Live performances and documentaries covering the evolution of jazz and beyond  representing funk, soul, hip-hop, folk, indie, electronic, blues, and other eclectic world genres.

name: Neurosurgical Atlas
description: Comprehensive database featuring neurosurgical cases, images, videos, models, and lectures.

name: Vanderbilt Library Primary Source Collections
description: Collection guides, or finding aids, to the archival collections held by Vanderbilt University Special Collections and University Archives, the History of Medicine Collection, and the Scarritt Bennett Center. Finding aids describe the context, arrangement, and structure of archival materials, allowing users to identify and request materials relevant to their research.

name: Gale In Context: Environmental Studies
description: Interdisciplinary resource for environmental and sustainability studies. Content includes reference works, case studies, multimedia materials, academic journals, newspapers, and magazines. Explore issues and events through topic, organization, and country portals.

name: Gale OneFile: Contemporary Womens Issues
description: Timely and historically relevant content on issues that influence womens lives across the globe. Topics include civil rights, health, education, professional development, entrepreneurship, and more.

name: Exploring Race in Society
description: Scholarly journals and essays that explore the complexities of race, ethnicity, diversity, and inclusivity, bringing new perspectives on the diverse experiences of racial groups throughout the world.

name: Platino Educa
description: Spanish and Ibero-American movies, covering themes across the environment, social sciences, language and literature, arts, and social justice.

name: PolicyMap
description: Data and mapping tool that provides access to over 15,000 indicators related to demographics, housing, crime, mortgages, health, jobs and more. Data is available at all common geographies (address, block group, census tract, zip code, county, city, state, MSA) as well as unique geographies like school districts and political boundaries. Create, save, download, customized maps and tables, or upload your own data to map.

name: Kotar Library
description: Hebrew language scholarly books and reference materials covering Israeli and Jewish studies, literature, history, education, and sociology, as well as other topics in the humanities and social sciences.

name: World Cities
description: Full text of surveys, budgets, statistical records, case studies, planning documents, training manuals, policy guidelines, reports, and news from the worlds top cities, capital cities with populations greater than 20,000 people, major cities in the Global South, and cities with particular research importance - such as those dealing with climate change, political instability, or rapid growth.

name: South Asia Commons: History and Culture
description: Full-text repository, search engine and preservation tool for openly available books, magazines, reports, historical journals, video, audio, zines, newspapers, letters, diaries and other primary sources.

name: COVE Studio
description: Collaborative anthology maker and annotation tool that offers pre-coded texts from the public domain that may be included in course anthologies and reading lists. You can also upload your own texts for annotation. At the Sign In drop down, select Login with Vanderbilt University.

meta.more_info: Vendor Tutorials: A COVE How To Guide and Teacher Guide are available.

name: Trismegistos
description: Interdisciplinary portal of papyrological and epigraphical resources that provides information about ancient texts (roughly BC 800 - 800 AD) from Egypt and the Nile valley in Greek, Latin, Egyptian, and other languages.

name: Engineering Workbench
description: Engineering standards from virtually any standards organization (e.g., ISO, IEEE, ASME). Register and sign in with your Vanderbilt email address to view subscribed standards or request new ones.

name: Environment Complete
description: Journals and monographs covering agriculture, ecosystem ecology, energy, public policy, sustainability and other related subjects.

name: Books of Modern China
description: Late Qing and Republican e-books including many rare titles. Books are full-text searchable and include author biographies.

name: Picture Gallery of Chinese Modern Literature
description: Contains more than one million images that have been collected from books, periodicals, newspapers, and old photos held by the Shanghai Library.

name: ProQuest One Education
description: Full-text scholarly journals, videos, dissertations, reference work, grey literature, popular news sources, and more are supplemented by topic pages covering educational theory and policy, pedagogy, key theorists, and research concepts.

name: Pediatric Surgery NaT
description: Database on pediatric surgery topics created by pediatric surgeons. Contains topic information, images, and surgical videos. Users can create a free personal account while on campus to favorite items and save searches.

name: AccessAPN
description: Developed in partnership with Sigma Theta Tau, this comprehensive online resource is dedicated to the education of advanced practice nursing students and includes books, cases, and review questions. Please provide feedback on this trial via https://forms.office.com/r/aa7J6zQwKX .

name: AccessPediatrics
description: Provides access to information essential for completing evaluation, diagnosis, and case management decisions, as well as for pursuing research or self-assessment and board review. Includes textbooks, cases, and review questions.

name: Advanced Practice Nursing Health Library
description: Textbooks, case studies, and quizzes for the education of advanced practice nursing students. Please provide feedback on this trial via https://forms.office.com/r/aa7J6zQwKX .

name: Osmosis: Nurse Practitioner (Student version)
description: Videos and quizzes for the education of advanced practice nursing students. Users must create personal accounts using their vanderbilt.edu email to access the content. Please provide feedback on this trial via https://forms.office.com/r/aa7J6zQwKX .

name: Refugees, Relief, and Resettlement: Forced Migration and World War II
description: Chronicles the plight of refugees and displaced persons across Europe, North Africa, and Asia, bringing together pamphlets, ephemera, government documents, relief organization publications, and refugee reports that recount the causes, effects and responses to refugee crises before, during and shortly after World War II.

name: Sage Business Data Decisions
description: Data challenge exercises provide students hands-on experience in applying fundamentals and data analysis methods to real-world scenarios through text, visualizations, interactive quizzes, and downloadable quantitative and qualitative data sets.

name: Massinvestor
description: Provides profiles on over 5,000 venture capital and private equity firms across the U.S. Lists of firms can be downloaded in Excel and PDF formats.

name: Latin American History and Culture
description: Books, magazines, photographs, maps, letters, diaries, ephemera, videos, and audio files digitized from Latin American and Latinx culture archives scattered across the world.

name: ProQuest One Anthropology
description: Ethnographic primary sources, including field notes, photographs, manuscripts, and key and unique video ethnographies, covering over 1,000 cultural groups globally. Supports anthropological research and teaching with curriculum-aligned resources such as video segments, lesson plans, and exercises.

name: ProQuest One Entertainment & Popular Culture
description: Archival and current resources spanning music, film, television, gaming, comics, and childhood and youth culture. Includes respected and influential industry publications, popular magazines, iconic film scripts, and engaging video content.

name: ProQuest One Global Studies & International Relations
description: Government documents, archival materials, periodicals, news scripts, multimedia, and a range of primary and secondary sources support in-depth study of global dynamics, ideologies and identity, governance, human rights, and social change.

name: ProQuest One History
description: Authoritative primary sources, scholarly articles, and documents that cover key events, figures, and time periods for researching social, political, and economic history across the globe.

name: ProQuest One Literature
description: Primary literary texts, ebooks, reference sources, full-text journals, dissertations, videos, and more, provide access to historical and contemporary works by celebrated and lesser-known authors worldwide including rare texts, and non-traditional sources such as comics and author readings.

name: ProQuest One Performing Arts
description: Multi-media content that connects foundational works  such as plays, performances, and scores  with secondary materials including journal articles, publications, and reference resources.Integrating varied content from classical music scores to dance videos, historical performance recordings, theatrical productions, and drama texts, enables deeper exploration of technical artistry, historical context, and cultural significance across different time periods.

name: ProQuest One Visual Arts & Design
description: Provides key indexes, and leading scholarly titles chronicling significant movements, trends, and figures in art, design, architecture, photography, and fashion.

name: ProQuest One Women's Studies
description: Primary and secondary sources in the areas of women's studies, gender and sexuality studies, LGBTQ+ studies, and men's and masculinity studies. New for 2026: fully incorporates GenderWatch content.

name: Lex Machina
description: Legal analytics tool that delivers data-driven insights to support litigation and legal strategy and analyzes vast amounts of court data. It transforms information into structured reports and visualization tools. Users' Lexis credentials should be used to log in.

meta.more_info: Lexis access questions can be answered by contacting Kyle Turner ( thomas.k.turner@vanderbilt.edu ).

name: MADCAD.com
description: Provides access to building codes, standards, and related documents for students and professionals in the architecture, engineering, and construction (AEC) industries.

name: Consensus
description: Academic AI search engine aggregating data from Semantic Scholar, OpenAlex, and a crawl of the scholarly web to cover nearly all of the highest-impact journals and the entirety of PubMed (over 200 million peer-reviewed documents). Note: the results from a Consensus search are not meant to be taken as a final truth but instead represent a snapshot of relevant research findings. Create an account using your vanderbilt.edu email address to access Pro features, which include expanded search capabilities. Trial access expires September 29, 2026. Please provide your feedback on this resource via this brief survey .

name: Compass
description: Offers legal professionals in-depth data and rankings on firms and lawyers, helping with market analysis, benchmarking, recruitment, and business growth. The platform provides key insights into financial results, diversity, and hiring trends in the legal field.

name: Statista  eCommerce Insights (ECDB)
description: Market intelligence and insights into online sales data from companies across the world. Includes profiles of retailers and markets, key performance indicators for transactions, company contacts, and white papers.

name: SciFinder-n
description: Provides integrated access to authoritative references, substances, and reactions in chemistry and related sciences. New Scifinder platform with PatentPak and MethodsNow.

meta.more_info: Access Note: Users of legacy Scifinder can use their existing username/password to access Scifinder-N. To register, visit: https://www.library.vanderbilt.edu/restricted/scifinder.php .

name: Digitalia Hispnica
description: Full-text Spanish language electronic books and journals. Users will need to create a free account to download content.

name: Maimonides' Medical Works Online
description: Provides critical editions of all surviving medical works by the famous rabbi, philosopher and medical doctor Moses Maimonides (1138-1204). Brings together the original texts and translations of all Maimonides surviving medical works as translated and edited by Gerrit Bos and a host of other experts in the field.

name: Zhonghua jing dian gu ji ku |
description: This database contains the standard punctuated versions of many important Chinese classical texts in the fields of philosophy, history, and literature as published by Zhonghua Shuju and other major publishers of punctuated editions. User Limit: 2 simultaneous users

name: BiblioPlanet
description: Collection of ebooks on Russian Literature and Linguistics, History, Law, Economics, Social Sciences and Culture. Vanderbilt users do not have access to Art Portal, Education Multimedia and Audiobooks.

name: Bloomsbury Theology Library
description: Contains key theological texts, includes primary sources and supporting articles. For titles with audio, click on the screen to see the controls.

name: Library of Catholic Thought
description: Includes resources for studying the life of Jesus and his impact on history and culture. For titles with audio, click on the screen to see the controls.

name: Airiti Library =
description: Publication in Taiwan platform, including Airiti iRead eBooks, Chinese Electronic Theses and Dissertation Service, Taiwan Electronic Periodical Services and National Palace Museum publications.

name: CNKI China Reference Works Online Database
description: Chinese language reference works including dictionaries, encyclopedia, subject dictionaries, industry directories, and handbooks. Simplified Chinese Interface also available .

name: Magazine Archives (EBSCOHost)
description: Collection of popular magazine archives.

meta.more_info: Ebony Magazine Archive, Life Magazine Archive, Sports Illustrated Magazine Archive, The Atlantic Magazine Archive, The Nation Magazine Archive, Time Magazine Archive, The National Review Archive, Architectural Digest Magazine Archive, Maclean's Magazine Archive, People Magazine Archive, The New Republic Magazine Archive, U.S. News and World Report Magazine Archive, Vanity Fair Magazine Archive, Jet Magazine Archive

name: FactSet
description: Financial platform that provides comprehensive data and analytics on global financial markets, economic measures, and companies. Any current Vanderbilt student, faculty or staff member can access FactSet for academic and non-commercial use from computers in the Walker Management Library.

name: Foreign Broadcast Information Service (FBIS) Daily Reports
description: FBIS Daily Reports issued by the U.S. Government, includes translations of broadcasts, news agency transmissions, newspapers, periodicals, and government statements from nations around the world. Current coverage includes 1941-1996.

name: AccessScience
description: Online resource containing reference material covering all major scientific disciplines.

name: Scopus
description: Provides abstract and citation information for peer-reviewed journals in the subject fields of life sciences, social sciences, physical sciences and health sciences.

name: PsycTests
description: APA PsycTests is a full text and multimedia database of psychological tests from the early 1900s to the present. Test records include descriptive summaries of the test and its development and administration, mostly drawn from articles in peer-reviewed journals or books, with downloadable tests. 75% of the test records contain the actual test or test items.

name: Covidence
description: Covidence is a systematic reviews production tool for title/abstract screening, full-text screening, data abstraction, and quality assessment. Covidence was designed by researchers familiar with the systematic review process to make conducting reviews more efficient. Vanderbilt users need to register for an account. You can find more information here

name: BrillOnline Bibliographies
description: The collection contains: Book History Online, Index Islamicus, and The Boston College Jesuit Bibliography.

name: SimplyAnalytics
description: A mapping, analytics, and data visualization application that makes it easy for anyone to create interactive maps and reports using thousands of demographic, business, and marketing variables.

name: Guatemala Collection
description: The Guatemala Collection contains 5 centuries of historical documents from Guatemalan and Spanish archives on the indigenous history of the Kaqchikel. "These documents cover the joys (celebrations and festivals) and the woes (natural disasters, epidemics, and crime) of life in Sacatepquez. Though these records are largely penned by non-indigenous officials, they expose the strategies employed by the indigenous peoples to defenderse (protect themselves) and superarse (to improve their lot), as well as the ploys and policies of the elites to maintain their own power and exploit indigenous labor and lands. In addition to the original documents, the collection includes a series of secondary sources, academic analysis and commentary on the holdings and their implications." Judith Maxwell, Tulane. Documents are ordered by place, theme, and chronology.

name: Constellate
description: This product is being sunset and our access will cease in April of 2025. Please submit an Ask a Librarian ticket if you have any questions. JSTOR remains committed to supporting text and data mining projects.

name: Youth and Popular Culture Magazine Archive
description: Provides access to unique periodicals from 1940-present, highlighting topics and trends of youth culture such as fashion, rock and roll, sports, sexuality, dating, as well as youth portrayal in the media, giving insight to what has and continues to influence youth culture, especially during times of rapid social and cultural change in the U.S., the UK, and Canada.

name: Entertainment Industry Archive
description: A resource containing primary sources for studying the history of the film and entertainment industries, from the era of vaudeville and silent movies through to the 21st century. This collection includes trade magazines covering film, music, broadcasting and theater are included, together with film fan magazines and music press titles

name: Central Asia, Persia and Afghanistan, 1834-1922
description: Central Asia, Persia and Afghanistan, 1834-1922: From Silk Road to Soviet Rule - collection of British Foreign Office diplomatic correspondence and memoranda provide insights not only into the impact of politics on the region, but also the regions peoples, cultures and societies.

name: Colonial Caribbean
description: Covers the history of the various territories under British colonial governance including administrative documentation, trade and shipping records, minutes of council meetings, and details of plantation life, colonial settlement, imperial rivalries across the region, and the growing concern of absentee landlords.

name: Gilded Age and Progressive Era
description: Covering Americas transformative age of industrialization, expanding wealth, inequality and social change. Personal collections, business records and rich visual content offer fresh perspectives on this influential period.

name: Henle Campus Edition
description: Digital catalog of music scores in an app available on iPad and Android tablets.  Users need to register for an account at https://www.henle-library.com/en/campus-edition  After registering, download the Henle Library app on your iPad or tablet (note: the app is not available for phones).  Users must revalidate their account every 3 months to maintain access. If an account is not revalidated, all data associated with that account will be lost.

name: EIU: Economist Intelligence Unit Country Reports Archive
description: Covering almost 200 countries, each report presents detailed statistics alongside expert commentary and forecasting from the EIUs analysts. This database presents the historical reports up to 1995, with all data from the statistical tables fully captured and downloadable in spreadsheet form. It is a unique archive of analysis and explanation of political, economic and commercial developments, together with historical statistical data.

name: Children's Literature Comprehensive Database
description: Provides book reviews, thematic lists, award listings, and curriculum tools for preK -12 media. Content includes fiction/non-fiction titles, reviews including author/title links, lesson plans and teaching guides.

name: Political Extremism and Radicalism
description: Diverse mixture of materials by and about both far-right and radical left political groups spanning the pre-war period into the twenty-first century. It contains a diverse range of content, including campaign materials, propaganda, government records, and various ephemera.

name: Access World News Research Collection
description: Research diverse perspectives, topics and trends that align with curricular areas such as Political Science, English, Sociology, Humanities, Business, International Studies and more. Features reliable, credible information from a wide variety of international, national and local news sources.

name: The Transatlantic Slave Trade, 1675-1907
description: Documents the British Slave Trade through records of slave traders and owners of the era.

name: Fo jiao jing dian ku
description: Also known as Database of Buddhist classics: provides full-text access to collections of Buddhist scriptures. It has the scans of the original scriptures can be viewed side-by-side with the machine readable text.

name: Diao long: Zhong Ri gu ji quan wen zi liao ku  : .
description: A database consisting of sub-databases containing the full-text of more than 30,000 pre-modern works, chiefly in Chinese, with some Japanese titles. The sub-databases include works such as the Daoist classics, the Sibu congkan and Sibu beiyao, manuscripts from Dunhuang, the Siku quanshu, the Xuxiu Siku quanshu, banned books from the Siku quanshu, local gazeteers, Imperial examination papers, folk literature, books on pharmacology and medicine, and more.

name: Proquest One Black Studies
description: Scholarly essays, recent periodicals, historical newspaper articles, reference books. Includes The Schomburg Studies on the Black Experience, Index to Black Periodicals Full Text, Black Literature Index, and the Chicago Defender historical newspaper.

name: TeenBookCloud
description: A digital collection of books for middle and high school age learners. Books are available in subjects across the curriculum. The collection also includes graphic novels, audio books, and enhanced novels. Lesson plans and quizzes are available for teachers. An app download is available for this resource. For login information, please contact David Golann at david.golann@vanderbilt.edu

name: TumbleBookLibrary
description: Digital book collection for young readers and educators. The collections includes animated story books, read alongs, chapter books, graphic novels, and non-fiction books. Lesson plans and quizzes are available for teachers. An app download is available for this resource. For login information, please contact David Golann at david.golann@vanderbilt.edu

name: TumbleMath
description: Comprehensive collection of math books for elementary age learners.

name: Quan wen fa xian: wen xian chuan shu xi tong  :
description: Includes more than 300,000 full-text Chinese classics, archives, and documents.

name: Pivot-RP
description: An expert-curated source of global funding opportunities with powerful workflow, intelligence and discovery tools

name: CHANT (Chinese Ancient Texts)
description: Comprehensive database of ancient Chinese texts from Pre-Han to the Six Dynasties.

name: East View Global Press Archive
description: A comprehensive collection of digital news sources from around the world encompassing newspapers in more than 30 language.

name: Gale Digital Scholar Lab
description: Online tool for collecting and analyzing data sets comprised of content from the Vanderbilt Libraries Gale Primary Sources databases. Text-mining methods include Ngrams, Named Entity Recognition, Topic Modeling, Parts of Speech, Sentiment Analysis, and Clustering. First-time users are required to create a personal account using either a Google or Microsoft account.

name: Ditki
description: Anatomy and physiology course with recorded lectures, figures, highlighting capability, flashcards, and review questions, First time users will need to create an account using their Vanderbilt email at https://ditki.com/register/group. Returning users, please visit: https://ditki.com/login

name: Hotshot
description: A learning platform for legal professionals, which provides videos and short summaries of important topics. Hotshots specialty is videos that are short and practical, accompanied by interactive quizzes and written materials, and each is about 15 minutes long, broken into short sections of a few minutes each. Each video comes with an outline-style summary that you can use to review the materials before watching any videos. Students, faculty, and staff will access by creating an account using their Vanderbilt email.

name: ProQuest TDM Unlimited
description: ProQuest TDM Studio is a web-based platform that allows you to analyze large amounts of text data from any of our ProQuest subscription content, including newspapers and journals. It allows you to either use your preferred data analysis methods in a coding workbench in Jupyter Notebook environment, or a pre-defined data visualization module with no coding experience needed. All users must create an individual account with their Vanderbilt email address. You will receive a welcome email and link to set up a one-on-one consultation with their expert support staff.

name: Sixteenth Century Pamphlets Online
description: German and Latin pamphlets from the Holy Roman Empire, concerned with the early Reformation movement, the Peasants' War, the Turkish wars, the status of Calvinists and Zwinglians in the Holy Roman Empire, propaganda against the papacy and the Jesuits, intra-Protestant theological quarrels, witch-hunting, and anti-Jewish polemics.

name: BioFinder
description: Biofinder is a comprehensive life sciences offering designed to accelerate drug discovery research by integrating biology and chemistry data with predictive models. SciFinder-n login is needed to access BioFinder. To register, visit: https://www.library.vanderbilt.edu/restricted/scifinder.php

name: Midpage
description: Legal research platform with AI integration. Law students can register using Vanderbilt email.

name: PowerNotes
description: Digital toolkit with AI integration to help with research and note-taking. Law students can register using Vanderbilt email.

name: Nature Master Classes
description: Online courses and workshops to provide professional development and training for researchers. More information can be found here Registration with an @vanderbilt.edu email address is required to access content.

name: Cortellis Drug Discovery Intelligence
description: CDDI is an integrated source of biological, chemical, and pharmacological data from a variety of sources, e.g., patent offices, conference and journal publications, regulatory agencies, and entity web pages, into a single drug discovery platform. Instructions: Please submit through the Eskind Library's Ask Biomedical form . Requests will be routed to the CDDI seat holder, who will work with you to deliver the needed insights using CDDI and other relevant information resources. Turnaround time will vary based on the complexity of the question. Please indicate in the question field of the form that it is related to CDDI or drug discovery, and state if there is an urgent deadline. For most inquiries, an initial response regarding the timeline will be received within 2 business days, if not sooner. More information: https://clarivate.com/life-sciences-healthcare/research-development/discovery-development/cortellis-pre-clinical-intelligence/ .

name: Legora
description: AI-powered legal technology platform designed to assist lawyers and law students with drafting, reviewing, and analyzing legal documents. It leverages large language models to support contract analysis, clause comparison, document summarization, and research workflows within a secure environment tailored for legal practice.

name: Dao jiao jing dian ku
description: Also known as Database of Taoist classics: includes Zhengtong Daozang (), Ming Wali xu Daozang () and Zang wai dao shu ()

name: Biomedical & Life Sciences Collection
description: A collection of multi-media lectures by world leading authorities on the fundamentals and latest developments in biomedicine and life sciences.

name: ClinicalKey Student
description: Collection of textbooks, images, and videos covering medical topics. Also includes exam prep questions. Users must register for an account to access the content. Access is limited to MD students.

meta.more_info: To create a login, click the "Login via your institution" and follow steps.

name: JoVe
description: Peer-reviewed videos of scientific experiments that show the details of the experiments.

name: Dalloz.fr
description: Provides access to legal materials published by Dalloz - codes, jurisprudence (case law), journals, and legal encyclopedias. All materials are searchable in French only.

name: Scite
description: An AI enhanced literature search and analysis tool with a smart citation index that uses scientific publications to contextualize citations and indicate whether the content provides supporting or contrasting evidence for a referenced work. It offers a quantitative and qualitative insight into how scientific publications cite each other. Provides access to the Scite Assistant, which is an LLM RAG tool that allows users to access and summarize millions of academic works.

meta.more_info: Access Note: Users must register for a personal account in order to use advanced features (create a custom dashboard, create a notification, save searches, etc.)

name: Kritisches Lexikon zur deutschsprachigen Gegenwartsliteratur-KLG
description: Bio-critical essays on contemporary German-speaking authors of the 20th and 21st centuries. Each entry Includes bibliographies of the authors works and secondary literature.

name: Ainosco Search
description: Provides access to a full-text search across book content and full access to specific ebooks published in Taiwan, Mainland China, Hong Kong, and Macau.

name: PLI Plus
description: Complete access to Practising Law Institutes treatises, course handbooks, legal forms, program transcripts and answer books. Full-text library dates back to 1980. Formerly, Discover Plus.

name: Art Song Transpositions
description: Digital sheet music database of 13,000+ arts songs and baroque arias available in multiple keys. Request a new key, new repertoire, and download existing repertoire. All transpositions are edited by musicians.

name: West African Magazines (Africa Commons)
description: West African Magazines includes arts, culture, and literary magazines from the 1930s to present. Published cultural commentary, fiction, poetry, reviews, and more from Ghana, Liberia, Nigeria, Senegal, and other countries highlight both pre-independence and post-independence eras.

name: APA Style Manual
description: APA Style is the referencing and publication style created and supported by the American Psychological Association. It is an author-date style, which means you use the surnames of the authors and the date of publication in text, and the reference list is in alphabetical order. This guide will assist with formatting and citation involved in this style. Let us know what you think by submitting your feedback: https://forms.office.com/r/22L2MYvs0x .

name: Harvey.ai
description: A generative AI platform for lawyers that helps with drafting, researching, and analysis by working within legal workflows and leveraging large language models tailored for the profession. All law students and faculty can log in using your Vanderbilt email address. Please contact Professor Kyle Turner, if you need assistance.

name: AccessEmergency Medicine
description: Provides access to emergency medicine textbooks, procedural videos, review questions, and cases.

name: Legaltech Hub
description: Provides insight, analysis, and reports into tools available in the legal technology landscape.

name: JapanKnowledge Lib
description: Encyclopedia, dictionaries, reference compilations, and periodical resources from Japan. Please send comments to Wanheng Li (wanheng.li@vanderbilt.edu).

name: UK Parliamentary & Government Papers
description: This database allows you to cross-search more than 300,000 parliamentary papers from the U.K. Parliament, Scottish Parliament, Scottish Government, Northern Ireland Assembly, Welsh Parliament, and other non-parliamentary reports.
